 APPENDICES


---


 Appendix A: XAUUSD Specific Parameters


 Typical Daily Range (ADR) for Gold
- Low volatility: $15-$25
- Average: $25-$40
- High volatility (news days): $40-$80+


 Typical Stop Loss Sizes by Timeframe
- 5M CRT: $2-$4
- 15M CRT: $3-$6
- 1H CRT: $5-$10
- 4H CRT: $8-$15
- Daily CRT: $12-$25


 Optimal Trading Hours for Gold CRT
- Primary: 07:00-10:00 GMT (London Open)
- Secondary: 13:00-16:00 GMT (NY Open)
- Avoid: 21:00-05:00 GMT (Low liquidity)
- Caution: 16:00-21:00 GMT (London close / late NY — thin liquidity)


 Key Gold Correlations
- DXY (Dollar Index): Inverse correlation (~-0.7 to -0.9) — if DXY sweeps a high and reverses, gold likely sweeps a low and reverses (CRT alignment)
- US 10Y Yields: Inverse correlation — rising yields = bearish gold
- Silver (XAGUSD): Direct correlation (~0.85) — can confirm CRT setups
- S&P 500: Variable correlation — risk-on/risk-off dynamics
- USDJPY: Moderate inverse — can provide confluence


---


 Appendix B: Glossary of CRT Terms


| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| CRT | Candle Range Theory — trading the internal structure of higher TF candles |
| Parent Candle | The higher timeframe candle whose range you're trading |
| Child Timeframe | The lower timeframe used for entry and execution |
| Judas Swing | The fake/manipulation move before the true direction |
| MSS | Market Structure Shift — confirmation that the manipulation is over |
| FVG | Fair Value Gap — imbalance left by displacement, used as entry zone |
| CE | Consequent Encroachment — the midpoint (50%) of an FVG |
| BSL | Buyside Liquidity — buy stops resting above highs |
| SSL | Sellside Liquidity — sell stops resting below lows |
| Displacement | An aggressive, impulsive candle showing institutional intent |
| Sweep | Price moving through a liquidity level before reversing |
| EQH | Equal Highs — double tops that attract buyside liquidity |
| EQL | Equal Lows — double bottoms that attract sellside liquidity |
| PDH/PDL | Previous Day High / Previous Day Low |
| PWH/PWL | Previous Week High / Previous Week Low |
| OB | Order Block — last opposite candle before a displacement |
| Killzone | Specific time windows where high-probability setups form |
| Premium | Above the 50% level of a range (expensive — look for sells) |
| Discount | Below the 50% level of a range (cheap — look for buys) |
| OTE | Optimal Trade Entry — the 62%-79% Fibonacci zone |
| MMBM | Market Maker Buy Model — the macro bullish CRT pattern |
| MMSM | Market Maker Sell Model — the macro bearish CRT pattern |
| Breaker Block | A failed order block that becomes support/resistance |
| Dealing Range | The current active swing high to swing low being traded |


---


 Appendix C: Recommended Learning Path


 Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2)
- Read this book in full
- Understand every term and concept
- Study 50 historical daily candles on XAUUSD — can you identify the manipulation and true move on each?


 Phase 2: Pattern Recognition (Weeks 3-4)
- Mark levels on XAUUSD daily (PDH, PDL, Asian H/L)
- Watch London and NY opens WITHOUT trading
- Identify CRT setups in real-time but don't enter
- Journal what you observe


 Phase 3: Backtesting (Weeks 5-8)
- Backtest 100+ CRT setups on XAUUSD
- Use TradingView replay mode
- Track statistics meticulously
- Refine your entry method, stop placement, and targets based on data


 Phase 4: Demo Trading (Weeks 9-12)
- Trade CRT on XAUUSD in demo account
- Follow all rules exactly as described
- Goal: 50+ demo trades with positive expectancy


 Phase 5: Small Live Trading (Weeks 13-16)
- Trade with minimal lot size (0.01 lots)
- Focus on process, not profit
- Goal: Prove the strategy works with real money psychology


 Phase 6: Scaling (Months 4+)
- Gradually increase position size as confidence and consistency grow
- Never increase risk % — only increase lot size as account grows
- Continue journaling and reviewing forever


---


 Appendix D: Final Words


CRT is not a "get rich quick" strategy. It is a framework for understanding how institutional players engineer price movement to fill their orders. When you understand that every candle tells a story — a story of accumulation, manipulation, and distribution — you stop being the victim of market manipulation and start being its beneficiary.


Gold is the ultimate CRT instrument. Its volatility, liquidity, and session-based structure make it the perfect canvas for this methodology. But gold is also unforgiving — it punishes impatience, greed, and lack of discipline with swift and painful losses.


Master the process:
1. Mark your levels
2. Wait for the sweep
3. Confirm the displacement
4. Verify the MSS
5. Enter at the FVG
6. Manage the trade
7. Repeat


Do this consistently, day after day, and CRT on XAUUSD will become not just a strategy, but a skill — a way of reading the market that most retail traders never develop.


The market will always try to deceive you. CRT teaches you to see through the deception.


Trade well. Trade patiently. Trade with discipline.
























 PART ELEVEN: TDS — TIME, DIRECTION, STRUCTURE


---


 Chapter 31: Introduction to TDS


 31.1 What is TDS?


TDS stands for Time, Direction, Structure — a three-pillar framework that acts as the pre-filter and alignment system for CRT execution. While CRT tells you HOW to trade (the mechanics of candle range manipulation), TDS tells you WHEN, WHERE, and WHY to trade.


Think of it this way:


> CRT is the weapon. TDS is the targeting system.


Without TDS, you are firing CRT trades blindly — taking every sweep, every displacement, every MSS as a potential trade. With TDS, you are selecting only the highest probability CRT setups that have time alignment, directional confirmation, and structural justification.


 31.2 The Three Pillars Defined


| Pillar | Question It Answers | What It Does |
|--------|-------------------|--------------|
| TIME | "When should I be looking for a trade?" | Filters out low-probability time windows |
| DIRECTION | "Which direction should I trade?" | Eliminates counter-trend mistakes |
| STRUCTURE | "Where in the market's structure am I?" | Confirms whether the price location supports the trade |


 31.3 Why TDS is Essential for XAUUSD


Gold is a 24-hour market with extreme volatility. Without TDS:


- You might take a beautiful CRT setup during Asian session — which has a 30% success rate on gold
- You might take a bearish CRT during a weekly bullish expansion — fighting the macro trend
- You might enter a CRT long when price is in premium territory on the daily — buying expensive


TDS eliminates these errors systematically.


 31.4 The TDS-CRT Integration Model


```
STEP 1: TIME → Is this the right time to trade?
         │
         ├── NO → Do NOT look for setups. Walk away.
         └── YES ↓
         
STEP 2: DIRECTION → What direction should I trade?
         │
         ├── UNCLEAR → Do NOT trade. Wait for clarity.
         └── CLEAR (Bullish or Bearish) ↓
         
STEP 3: STRUCTURE → Does the current price structure support a trade in this direction?
         │
         ├── NO → Do NOT trade. Structure conflicts.
         └── YES ↓
         
STEP 4: CRT EXECUTION → Now apply the CRT mechanics
         │
         ├── Wait for liquidity sweep
         ├── Confirm displacement
         ├── Confirm MSS
         ├── Enter at FVG
         └── Manage trade per CRT rules
```


The key insight: TDS comes BEFORE CRT. You do not even look for CRT setups until all three TDS pillars are aligned.


---


 PILLAR ONE: TIME


---


 Chapter 32: The TIME Pillar — Deep Dive


 32.1 Time is the Most Underrated Edge


Most retail traders focus on price. Professionals focus on time first, then price.


Why? Because:


1. Institutional algorithms are time-based — they activate at specific times
2. Liquidity is time-dependent — it concentrates during specific windows
3. Volatility is cyclical — it expands and contracts predictably based on time
4. CRT manipulation follows a time schedule — the Judas Swing doesn't happen randomly


For XAUUSD specifically:
- Gold has the most predictable time-based behavior of any instrument
- The session structure (Asian → London → NY) creates a daily rhythm
- Ignoring time is the single biggest reason traders fail on gold


 32.2 The Four Time Dimensions in TDS


TDS breaks "Time" into four dimensions:


Dimension 1: Session Time
Dimension 2: Killzone Time
Dimension 3: Day of Week Time
Dimension 4: Time of Month / Macro Time


Each dimension acts as a filter — the more dimensions that align, the higher the probability.


---


 32.3 Dimension 1: Session Time


 The Three Sessions — Deep Analysis for Gold


ASIAN SESSION (00:00 - 08:00 GMT)


```
Character: The "Setup" Session
Role in CRT: ACCUMULATION phase
Gold behavior:
- Average range: $5-$15
- Low volume, tight spreads during Tokyo overlap
- Price typically consolidates or drifts slowly
- Creates the "range" that London will break
- Smart money positions are being built quietly


CRT role:
- Asian session HIGH and LOW become critical liquidity levels
- These levels WILL be swept during London or NY
- DO NOT trade CRT during Asian session on gold (exceptions below)


Exception: If a major Asian news event (Bank of Japan, Chinese data) 
creates a sharp move, a mini-CRT can form — but this is rare for gold
```


LONDON SESSION (07:00 - 16:00 GMT)


```
Character: The "Manipulation + True Move" Session
Role in CRT: MANIPULATION and DISTRIBUTION phases
Gold behavior:
- Average range: $15-$35
- Highest volume period for gold
- Most institutional participation
- London bullion fix occurs at 10:30 AM and 3:00 PM London time
- Price moves are decisive and directional


CRT role:
- 70%+ of daily CRT manipulation (Judas Swings) happen at London open
- The first 2-3 hours of London (07:00-10:00) are where CRT forms
- The rest of London (10:00-16:00) is where the CRT true move plays out


Priority: ★★★★★ (HIGHEST for CRT trading on gold)
```


NEW YORK SESSION (13:00 - 21:00 GMT)


```
Character: The "Continuation or Reversal" Session
Role in CRT: SECONDARY MANIPULATION or CONTINUATION of distribution
Gold behavior:
- Average range: $15-$40 (can exceed London on news days)
- Overlaps with London (13:00-16:00) = highest combined liquidity
- Major US economic data released at 13:30 GMT
- Can create NEW CRT setups independent of London


CRT role:
- If London established a CRT direction, NY often continues it
- If London's CRT move has completed (hit targets), NY can create a reversal CRT
- The London-NY overlap (13:00-16:00) creates the most volatile gold moves
- NY CRT setups are especially powerful on NFP/CPI/FOMC days


Priority: ★★★★ (HIGH for CRT trading on gold)
```


 Session Time Decision Matrix for Gold


| Current Session | Should I Look for CRT? | Probability |
|----------------|----------------------|-------------|
| Asian (00:00-07:00) | NO — mark levels only | N/A |
| Early London (07:00-08:00) | ALERT — manipulation may begin | Preparing |
| London Killzone (08:00-10:00) | YES — PRIMARY CRT window | HIGHEST |
| Mid London (10:00-13:00) | Monitor — true move developing | Moderate |
| London-NY Overlap (13:00-16:00) | YES — SECONDARY CRT window | HIGH |
| Late NY (16:00-19:00) | CAUTION — liquidity thinning | Low-Moderate |
| NY Close (19:00-21:00) | NO — avoid new positions | Low |
| After Hours (21:00-00:00) | NO — dead zone for gold | Very Low |


---


 32.4 Dimension 2: Killzone Time


Killzones are the specific micro-windows within sessions where CRT setups form most frequently.


 XAUUSD Killzones — Precise Timing


London Killzone: 07:00 - 10:00 GMT


```
Sub-windows:
07:00-07:30: Early birds — price may start drifting toward liquidity
07:30-08:00: Pre-manipulation — price approaches key level
08:00-08:30: ★ PRIME MANIPULATION WINDOW ★
              - This is where the Judas Swing peaks on gold
              - Highest frequency of liquidity sweeps
08:30-09:30: ★ DISPLACEMENT + MSS WINDOW ★
              - Reversal should be evident by now
              - FVG creation and MSS confirmation
09:30-10:00: Late entry window
              - If you missed the initial setup, 
                retracement to FVG may occur here
              - Last chance for CRT entry in London KZ


After 10:00: If no CRT has formed, London KZ is over
             Wait for NY KZ
```


New York Killzone: 12:30 - 15:00 GMT


```
Sub-windows:
12:30-13:00: Pre-market positioning
13:00-13:30: ★ NY OPEN MANIPULATION WINDOW ★
              - Second highest frequency of CRT Judas Swings
              - Especially potent if London didn't produce a clear CRT
13:30-14:00: ★ NEWS RELEASE WINDOW ★
              - US economic data (NFP, CPI, etc.)
              - News-driven CRT setups form here
14:00-15:00: Post-news displacement and entry
              - MSS confirmation window
              - FVG entries


After 15:00: NY KZ effectiveness drops significantly
             Avoid new CRT entries
```


Silver Bullet Window: 14:00 - 15:00 GMT (ICT specific)


```
This micro-window (called the "Silver Bullet" in ICT methodology) 
is specifically powerful for gold:
- Price often creates a clean FVG during this hour
- If you've been waiting for a setup all day, this is the last reliable window
- Works best when aligned with the day's CRT bias
```


 Killzone Probability Matrix for Gold


| Killzone | CRT Frequency | Win Rate | Average Move |
|----------|--------------|----------|--------------|
| London 08:00-08:30 | Very High | 60-70% | $15-$30 |
| London 08:30-09:30 | High | 55-65% | $10-$25 |
| NY 13:00-13:30 | High | 55-65% | $12-$28 |
| NY 13:30-14:00 (News) | High but chaotic | 50-60% | $20-$50 |
| Silver Bullet 14:00-15:00 | Moderate | 55-60% | $10-$20 |
| Other times | Low | 35-45% | Variable |


---


 32.5 Dimension 3: Day of Week Time


Each day of the week has its own "personality" for gold CRT trading.


 Monday — The "Range Builder"


```
Characteristics:
- Often the lowest volatility day for gold
- Weekly candle is just opening
- Institutions are assessing the week ahead
- Price often creates the weekly range extremes early in the week


CRT Behavior:
- Monday CRT setups are LOWER PROBABILITY
- The weekly manipulation (Judas Swing) often begins on Monday
- Monday may create a false move that gets reversed on Tuesday/Wednesday
- Best approach: Mark Monday's range, don't trade aggressively


TDS Rating: ★★ (Low priority for CRT)


Exception: If Friday created a massive move and Monday gaps or 
continues, a strong CRT setup can form
```


 Tuesday — The "Prime Day"


```
Characteristics:
- Statistically the HIGHEST PROBABILITY day for CRT on gold
- Monday's range has been established — Tuesday often sweeps it
- Institutional order flow picks up significantly
- London session CRT is especially reliable on Tuesdays


CRT Behavior:
- Tuesday often sweeps Monday's high OR low and reverses
- The weekly CRT Judas Swing frequently completes on Tuesday
- Tuesday-formed CRT trades often run into Wednesday
- Best day for fresh weekly CRT entries


TDS Rating: ★★★★★ (HIGHEST priority for CRT)
```


 Wednesday — The "Continuation Day"


```
Characteristics:
- Mid-week — often continues Tuesday's established direction
- FOMC announcements fall on Wednesdays (8 times per year)
- Can create secondary CRT setups
- If Tuesday's CRT trade is running, Wednesday often extends it


CRT Behavior:
- Non-FOMC Wednesdays: Continuation of Tuesday direction, 
  look for pullback CRT entries
- FOMC Wednesdays: Expect extreme CRT around 19:00 GMT announcement
  - Pre-FOMC consolidation = accumulation
  - Announcement spike = manipulation  
  - Post-FOMC move = distribution


TDS Rating: ★★★★ (HIGH — especially FOMC Wednesdays)
```


 Thursday — The "Pre-Position Day"


```
Characteristics:
- Traders positioning ahead of Friday
- Can create CRT setups as price seeks liquidity before weekend
- Weekly claims data released (moderate impact on gold)
- Often tests the week's range limits


CRT Behavior:
- Good CRT day if the week has been directional
- Price may create a "mini-CRT" within Thursday to set up Friday
- If Tuesday's CRT trade hasn't reached weekly targets, Thursday may provide re-entry
- Watch for London session CRT setups specifically


TDS Rating: ★★★ (MODERATE — depends on weekly context)
```


 Friday — The "Payoff Day"


```
Characteristics:
- NFP (first Friday of month) creates extreme CRT
- Non-NFP Fridays: Often profit-taking, reduced liquidity after London
- Weekly candle is closing — price seeks to complete the weekly CRT
- Volatility front-loaded in London/early NY, then dies


CRT Behavior:
- NFP Fridays: Classic news-driven CRT (spike and reverse)
  - THE SINGLE BEST DAY FOR CRT ON GOLD when NFP occurs
  - Pre-NFP range → NFP spike sweeps one side → reversal = CRT
- Non-NFP Fridays: Mixed results
  - Morning CRT can work, but afternoon often stalls
  - Avoid new CRT entries after 15:00 GMT on Fridays


TDS Rating: ★★★★★ (NFP Fridays) / ★★ (Non-NFP Fridays)
```


 Weekly Day Rating Summary


| Day | CRT Priority | Best Action |
|-----|-------------|-------------|
| Monday | ★★ | Mark ranges, observe, minimal trading |
| Tuesday | ★★★★★ | PRIMARY trading day — look for weekly CRT setup |
| Wednesday | ★★★★ | Continuation entries or FOMC trades |
| Thursday | ★★★ | Secondary entries if weekly move ongoing |
| Friday (NFP) | ★★★★★ | News CRT — highest volatility setup |
| Friday (non-NFP) | ★★ | Morning only, close positions early |


---


 32.6 Dimension 4: Time of Month / Macro Time


 Monthly CRT Cycle


```
Week 1 (Days 1-7):
- Monthly candle opening
- NFP typically falls here (first Friday)
- "Accumulation" phase of the monthly candle
- Mark previous month's high and low as key liquidity levels
- Monthly CRT Judas Swing may begin


Week 2 (Days 8-14):
- CPI data often released (around day 10-14)
- Monthly CRT manipulation phase often occurs
- Price may sweep the previous month's high or low
- Best week for identifying the monthly CRT direction


Week 3 (Days 15-21):
- FOMC meetings often fall here (mid-month)
- If monthly CRT direction is established, this week extends it
- "Distribution" phase of the monthly candle begins


Week 4 (Days 22-31):
- Month-end flows
- Portfolio rebalancing by institutions
- Monthly candle seeking closure
- Monthly CRT should be completing its true move
- Caution on last 2 days — thin liquidity and erratic moves
```


 Quarterly CRT Cycle for Gold


```
Month 1 of Quarter (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct):
- New quarterly flows
- Institutional reallocation
- Quarter opens — accumulation phase
- Look for quarterly CRT range to establish


Month 2 of Quarter (Feb, May, Aug, Nov):
- Manipulation month
- Price often sweeps Month 1's extreme
- This is where the quarterly Judas Swing occurs
- Be alert for major CRT setups on Weekly/Monthly


Month 3 of Quarter (Mar, Jun, Sep, Dec):
- Distribution month
- The true quarterly move plays out
- Strongest trends on gold often occur in Month 3
- Hold runners from Month 2 CRT entries
```


 32.7 The TIME Confluence Score


For each potential CRT trade, score the time alignment:


```
TIME SCORE CARD:


Session:
□ London KZ (07:00-10:00) → +3 points
□ NY KZ (13:00-15:00) → +2 points
□ London-NY overlap (13:00-16:00) → +2 points
□ Silver Bullet (14:00-15:00) → +1 point
□ Asian session → -2 points
□ Late NY (after 16:00) → -1 point


Day of Week:
□ Tuesday → +3 points
□ Wednesday (FOMC) → +3 points
□ Wednesday (non-FOMC) → +2 points
□ Thursday → +1 point
□ Friday (NFP) → +3 points
□ Friday (non-NFP) → -1 point
□ Monday → -1 point


Monthly Timing:
□ Week 2 (manipulation week) → +2 points
□ Week 3 (distribution week) → +1 point
□ Week 1 (accumulation week) → 0 points
□ Last 2 days of month → -1 point


SCORING:
6+ points → EXCELLENT TIME ALIGNMENT — proceed to Direction
3-5 points → ACCEPTABLE TIME ALIGNMENT — proceed with caution
0-2 points → MARGINAL — consider skipping
Below 0 → DO NOT TRADE — time is against you
```


---


 PILLAR TWO: DIRECTION


---


 Chapter 33: The DIRECTION Pillar — Deep Dive


 33.1 Direction is Not Opinion — It's Structure


The Direction pillar of TDS is about establishing an objective, evidence-based directional bias before looking for CRT setups.


> "I think gold is going up" is NOT a directional bias.
> "Gold has broken weekly structure to the upside, is above the weekly FVG, and the daily candle is seeking buyside liquidity" IS a directional bias.


 33.2 The Multi-Timeframe Direction Framework


Direction is determined by reading market structure from top to bottom:


```
MONTHLY DIRECTION
     ↓ feeds into
WEEKLY DIRECTION  
     ↓ feeds into
DAILY DIRECTION
     ↓ feeds into
4H DIRECTION
     ↓ feeds into
1H DIRECTION (execution bias)
```


Rule: Trade in the direction of the MAJORITY of timeframes. If Monthly, Weekly, and Daily are all bullish, your CRT trades should be bullish — period.


 33.3 How to Read Direction on Each Timeframe


 Reading Monthly Direction for Gold


```
BULLISH MONTHLY:
- Series of higher highs and higher lows on monthly
- Price is above the monthly 50% equilibrium of the current range
- Most recent monthly candle closed bullish
- Unmitigated buyside liquidity above (targets above)


BEARISH MONTHLY:
- Series of lower highs and lower lows on monthly
- Price is below the monthly 50% equilibrium
- Most recent monthly candle closed bearish
- Unmitigated sellside liquidity below (targets below)


NEUTRAL MONTHLY:
- Price is chopping within a range
- No clear HH/HL or LH/LL pattern
- Wait for clarity before establishing direction
```


 Reading Weekly Direction for Gold


```
BULLISH WEEKLY:
- Last completed weekly candle closed bullish
- Weekly structure shows HH/HL
- Price is in the discount zone (below 50%) of the weekly dealing range
  AND showing signs of reversal
  OR price is above 50% and expanding higher
- Nearest weekly liquidity is ABOVE (buyside)


BEARISH WEEKLY:
- Last completed weekly candle closed bearish
- Weekly structure shows LH/LL
- Price is in the premium zone (above 50%)
  AND showing signs of reversal
  OR price is below 50% and expanding lower
- Nearest weekly liquidity is BELOW (sellside)


KEY SIGNAL: Where did the weekly candle CLOSE relative to its range?
- Closed near the high → bullish direction for next week
- Closed near the low → bearish direction for next week
- Closed in the middle → unclear — be cautious
```


 Reading Daily Direction for Gold


```
This is the MOST IMPORTANT direction for CRT execution:


BULLISH DAILY:
- Last 2-3 daily candles show bullish pressure
- Price has swept sellside liquidity and reversed (CRT confirmed)
- Daily FVG is bullish and unmitigated below
- Price is respecting a daily order block
- Today's open is above yesterday's close


BEARISH DAILY:
- Last 2-3 daily candles show bearish pressure
- Price has swept buyside liquidity and reversed
- Daily FVG is bearish and unmitigated above
- Price is respecting a daily order block
- Today's open is below yesterday's close


CRITICAL DAILY SIGNALS FOR GOLD:
1. Did yesterday's candle sweep a key level?
2. Did yesterday's candle create an FVG?
3. Where did yesterday's candle close relative to its range?
4. Is today's opening price above or below yesterday's body?
```


 Reading 4H Direction for Gold


```
For intraday CRT trades, the 4H direction is your "steering wheel":


BULLISH 4H:
- Last 2-3 4H candles created higher lows
- Most recent 4H candle closed bullish
- 4H FVG is bullish and price is pulling back toward it
- 4H market structure has not been broken to the downside


BEARISH 4H:
- Last 2-3 4H candles created lower highs
- Most recent 4H candle closed bearish
- 4H FVG is bearish and price is pulling back toward it
- 4H market structure has not been broken to the upside


DIRECTION CHANGE ALERT:
- If 4H structure BREAKS against the daily direction,
  STOP TRADING until alignment is restored
- This is a "conflict zone" — CRT setups here have lower probability
```


 33.4 The Direction Alignment Matrix


```
STRONG BULLISH (Trade long CRT only):
Monthly: Bullish  ✓
Weekly:  Bullish  ✓
Daily:   Bullish  ✓
4H:      Bullish  ✓
→ Take ALL bullish CRT setups in killzones
→ Ignore ALL bearish CRT setups


MODERATE BULLISH (Trade long CRT with confirmation):
Monthly: Bullish  ✓
Weekly:  Bullish  ✓
Daily:   Bullish  ✓
4H:      Unclear/Bearish  ✗
→ Wait for 4H to realign with higher TFs
→ Only take bullish CRT if 4H shows MSS back to bullish


WEAK / CONFLICTING (Avoid or reduce size):
Monthly: Bullish  ✓
Weekly:  Bearish  ✗
Daily:   Bullish  ✓
4H:      Bearish  ✗
→ Mixed signals — this is a CHOP environment
→ Reduce position size by 50% or skip entirely


STRONG BEARISH (Trade short CRT only):
Monthly: Bearish  ✓
Weekly:  Bearish  ✓
Daily:   Bearish  ✓
4H:      Bearish  ✓
→ Take ALL bearish CRT setups in killzones
→ Ignore ALL bullish CRT setups
```


 33.5 Direction Determination Tools


 Tool 1: The Last Swing Analysis


On each timeframe, identify:
- The last significant swing high
- The last significant swing low
- Has the most recent price action broken the swing high (bullish) or swing low (bearish)?


```
Example on XAUUSD Daily:


Last Daily Swing High: $2,395
Last Daily Swing Low: $2,340


Current Price: $2,365


Analysis:
- Price is between swing high and swing low
- Neither has been broken
- Direction is NEUTRAL until one breaks


If price breaks above $2,395:
- Direction shifts BULLISH
- Now looking for bullish CRT setups (sweep of a low → reversal up)


If price breaks below $2,340:
- Direction shifts BEARISH
- Now looking for bearish CRT setups (sweep of a high → reversal down)
```


 Tool 2: The Candle Bias Reading


Read the last 3-5 candles on your bias timeframe:


```
Reading the last 5 Daily candles on XAUUSD:


Day 1: Bearish (large body, close near low)
Day 2: Bearish (lower close than Day 1)
Day 3: Doji (indecision — potential reversal?)
Day 4: Bullish (strong close near high — potential MSS)
Day 5: Bullish (higher close — confirmation)


Direction reading: 
- Days 1-2 were bearish
- Day 3 was transition
- Days 4-5 are bullish
- Direction is shifting from bearish to BULLISH
- Look for bullish CRT setups
```


 Tool 3: The Premium/Discount Check


```
Daily Dealing Range:
- Range High: $2,400
- Range Low: $2,320
- Range: $80
- 50% (Equilibrium): $2,360


Current Price: $2,345


Analysis:
- Price is BELOW 50% → in the DISCOUNT zone
- If direction is bullish → EXCELLENT — buying in discount
- If direction is bearish → price may go lower, but buying could be risky


CRT Application:
- In discount zone with bullish bias → look for CRT longs
- In premium zone with bearish bias → look for CRT shorts
- In discount zone with bearish bias → CONFLICT (be careful)
- In premium zone with bullish bias → CONFLICT (be careful)
```


 Tool 4: Institutional Positioning (COT Report)


The Commitment of Traders (COT) report, released weekly, shows how institutional players are positioned in gold futures:


```
Reading COT for Gold CRT Direction:


Large Speculators (Hedge Funds):
- Net Long and INCREASING → Bullish direction
- Net Long and DECREASING → Direction may be shifting bearish
- Net Short and INCREASING → Bearish direction
- Net Short and DECREASING → Direction may be shifting bullish


Commercial Hedgers (Bullion Banks):
- These are typically OPPOSITE to the trend
- When commercials are extremely net short → market may be near a top
- When commercials are extremely net long → market may be near a bottom


CRT Application:
- If COT shows increasing institutional longs → daily/weekly CRT should be bullish
- If COT shows extreme positioning → watch for macro CRT reversal
```


 33.6 The Direction Scoring System


```
DIRECTION SCORE CARD:


Monthly Structure:
□ Bullish → +2 for long bias / -2 for short bias
□ Bearish → +2 for short bias / -2 for long bias
□ Neutral → 0


Weekly Structure:
□ Bullish → +2 for long bias / -2 for short bias
□ Bearish → +2 for short bias / -2 for long bias
□ Neutral → 0


Daily Structure:
□ Bullish → +3 for long bias / -3 for short bias
□ Bearish → +3 for short bias / -3 for long bias
□ Neutral → 0


4H Structure:
□ Bullish → +2 for long bias / -2 for short bias
□ Bearish → +2 for short bias / -2 for long bias
□ Neutral → 0


Premium/Discount:
□ In discount zone for longs → +2
□ In premium zone for shorts → +2
□ Conflicting (discount for shorts, premium for longs) → -2


COT Report:
□ Supports your direction → +1
□ Conflicts with your direction → -1
□ Neutral → 0


SCORING (for your intended direction):
10+ → STRONG DIRECTIONAL ALIGNMENT — full confidence
7-9 → GOOD ALIGNMENT — proceed normally
4-6 → MODERATE ALIGNMENT — reduce size or wait
1-3 → WEAK ALIGNMENT — likely skip
0 or below → NO ALIGNMENT — DO NOT TRADE this direction
```


---


 33.7 Direction Conflicts and Resolution


 Conflict Type 1: Monthly Bullish, Weekly Bearish


```
Interpretation:
- The weekly is pulling back against the monthly trend
- This is likely a retracement within the larger uptrend
- The weekly pullback may be the "manipulation" of a monthly CRT


Resolution:
- DO NOT short gold aggressively
- Wait for the weekly bearish move to sweep a key weekly low
- Then look for bullish CRT on the daily/4H for re-entry into the monthly trend
- This is a "buy the dip" scenario via CRT
```


 Conflict Type 2: Daily Bullish, 4H Bearish


```
Interpretation:
- The 4H is correcting against the daily trend
- This is a normal intraday pullback
- The 4H pullback may be creating the CRT manipulation on the daily


Resolution:
- Wait for 4H to sweep a key 4H low
- Look for 4H MSS back to bullish
- This 4H MSS = entry into the daily bullish CRT
- Enter on the 15M/5M FVG after 4H MSS confirms
```


 Conflict Type 3: All Timeframes Bullish, but Price in Premium


```
Interpretation:
- Trend is strongly bullish but price is "expensive"
- Buying here has poor R:R even if direction is correct
- Need a pullback to discount for a good CRT entry


Resolution:
- Wait for a pullback to the 50% level or below
- The pullback itself may be a CRT manipulation (sweep of a low)
- Enter the CRT long when price reaches discount AND shows displacement
- If no pullback comes and price keeps rallying → accept you missed it, 
  don't chase
```


---


 33.8 Dynamic Direction — When Direction Changes


Direction is not static. It changes when key structural levels break. You must be prepared to adapt.


```
DIRECTION CHANGE PROTOCOL:


Level 1 Change (Minor — 4H):
- 4H swing point breaks against your bias
- Action: Pause trading, wait for 4H to realign or for daily to confirm change
- Do NOT immediately switch direction


Level 2 Change (Moderate — Daily):
- Daily swing point breaks against your bias
- Action: Close any open positions in the old direction
- Reassess direction from Weekly down
- May require waiting 1-2 days for new direction to establish


Level 3 Change (Major — Weekly):
- Weekly swing point breaks against your bias
- Action: Full directional reversal
- Mark new weekly structure levels
- Begin looking for CRT setups in the NEW direction


Level 4 Change (Macro — Monthly):
- Monthly structure breaks
- Action: Major repositioning
- This happens only a few times per year on gold
- Creates the biggest CRT opportunities of the year
```


---


 PILLAR THREE: STRUCTURE


---


 Chapter 34: The STRUCTURE Pillar — Deep Dive


 34.1 What is "Structure" in TDS?


Structure refers to the price framework — the arrangement of key levels, zones, and patterns that define where price is in the market's "map."


While Direction tells you WHICH WAY to trade, Structure tells you WHERE you are and whether your current position makes sense for a CRT trade.


 34.2 The Five Elements of Structure


```
Element 1: SWING STRUCTURE (Market Structure)
Element 2: LIQUIDITY STRUCTURE (Where are the stops?)
Element 3: IMBALANCE STRUCTURE (Where are the FVGs?)
Element 4: ORDER FLOW STRUCTURE (Order Blocks)
Element 5: RANGE STRUCTURE (Premium/Discount, Dealing Range)
```


Each element must be analyzed before taking a CRT trade.


---


 34.3 Element 1: Swing Structure


 Identifying Swing Points on XAUUSD


A swing high is a candle high with lower highs on both sides.
A swing low is a candle low with higher lows on both sides.


```
On XAUUSD Daily:


Valid Swing High:
      ★
     /  \
    /    \
   /      \
Previous candles had lower highs / Following candles have lower highs


Valid Swing Low:
   \      /
    \    /
     \  /
      ★
Previous candles had higher lows / Following candles have higher lows
```


 Swing Structure Types


```
BULLISH SWING STRUCTURE:
- Higher Highs (HH) and Higher Lows (HL)
- Each swing low holds above the previous swing low
- Each swing high exceeds the previous swing high


Visualization:
        HH
       /
      /
    HL
   /
  /
HL
```


```
BEARISH SWING STRUCTURE:
- Lower Highs (LH) and Lower Lows (LL)
- Each swing high fails to exceed the previous swing high
- Each swing low breaks below the previous swing low


Visualization:
LH
  \
   \
    LH
      \
       \
        LL
```


```
RANGING STRUCTURE:
- Swing highs and lows are roughly equal
- Price bouncing between support and resistance
- No clear HH/HL or LH/LL pattern


Visualization:
EQH -------- EQH
|              |
|              |
EQL -------- EQL
```


 How Swing Structure Affects CRT


```
BULLISH STRUCTURE + CRT:
- CRT manipulation should sweep a SWING LOW
- The sweep of the swing low is "manipulation within the uptrend"
- The CRT true move targets the next swing high or higher
- This is the highest probability CRT long setup


BEARISH STRUCTURE + CRT:
- CRT manipulation should sweep a SWING HIGH
- The sweep of the swing high is "manipulation within the downtrend"
- The CRT true move targets the next swing low or lower
- This is the highest probability CRT short setup


RANGING STRUCTURE + CRT:
- CRT can work in ranges — sweep the range low → target range high (and vice versa)
- But range CRT has lower probability than trending CRT
- Best used when you can identify which side of the range has MORE liquidity
```


---


 34.4 Element 2: Liquidity Structure


This is about mapping WHERE stops are resting in the market. Think of it as creating a "liquidity map" of XAUUSD.


 Building a Liquidity Map for Gold


```
STEP 1: Mark all obvious highs and lows on your chart


On XAUUSD 4H Chart:
- Mark every swing high and swing low from the last 2 weeks
- Mark PDH and PDL
- Mark PWH and PWL
- Mark equal highs and equal lows (double/triple tops and bottoms)
- Mark session highs and lows (Asian H/L, London H/L)
- Mark round numbers ($XX00, $XX50, $XX25, $XX75)


STEP 2: Grade each level by "liquidity density"


Grading system:
★★★★★ = Multiple confluent levels stacked together
          (e.g., PDH + EQH + round number at same price)
          → EXTREME liquidity — price WILL target this eventually


★★★★ = Two confluent levels
          (e.g., swing high + session high)
          → HIGH liquidity


★★★ = Single significant level
          (e.g., swing high or PDL alone)
          → MODERATE liquidity


★★ = Minor level
          (e.g., 1H swing point)
          → LOW liquidity


★ = Insignificant
          (e.g., random candle high/low)
          → Not worth marking


STEP 3: Identify which side has MORE unswept liquidity


If there's more unswept liquidity ABOVE price:
→ Price is likely to go UP to sweep it
→ Bullish CRT setups have targets
→ BUT first, price may DIP to sweep minor sellside liquidity (CRT manipulation down)


If there's more unswept liquidity BELOW price:
→ Price is likely to go DOWN to sweep it
→ Bearish CRT setups have targets
→ BUT first, price may RALLY to sweep minor buyside liquidity (CRT manipulation up)
```


 The "Liquidity Void" Concept


```
A Liquidity Void is an area on the chart where price moved rapidly 
without much trading — it's "empty" of resting orders.


On XAUUSD, liquidity voids appear:
- After news spikes (NFP, CPI)
- After strong displacement moves
- In overnight gaps (rare on gold but can happen on Monday open)


CRT and Liquidity Voids:
- Price tends to return to fill liquidity voids
- A CRT true move may target a liquidity void for completion
- If a liquidity void exists between your entry and target, 
  price should move through it quickly (less resistance)
- If a liquidity void exists between your entry and stop, 
  be careful — your stop might get hit quickly too
```


---


 34.5 Element 3: Imbalance Structure (FVG Mapping)


 Creating an FVG Map for Gold


```
STEP 1: On the Daily chart, mark all UNMITIGATED FVGs from the last month
STEP 2: On the 4H chart, mark all UNMITIGATED FVGs from the last week
STEP 3: On the 1H chart, mark all UNMITIGATED FVGs from the last 3 days


COLOR CODE:
- Bullish FVGs = Blue/Green zones
- Bearish FVGs = Red/Orange zones
- Mitigated (price has returned to them) = Grey (remove or dim)


IMPORTANCE:
- Daily FVGs are the MOST important — these are where price WILL return
- 4H FVGs are secondary — these guide intraday movement
- 1H FVGs are tertiary — these are execution-level entry zones
```


 FVG Structure and CRT Direction


```
Scenario: Multiple BULLISH FVGs below current price (unmitigated)


Interpretation:
- These FVGs act as "magnets" pulling price down
- Price may drop to mitigate (fill) these FVGs
- This DROP could be the CRT manipulation phase
- After filling the FVG, price reverses = CRT true move up


Scenario: Multiple BEARISH FVGs above current price (unmitigated)


Interpretation:
- These FVGs act as "magnets" pulling price up
- Price may rally to mitigate these FVGs
- This RALLY could be the CRT manipulation phase
- After filling the FVG, price reverses = CRT true move down
```


 XAUUSD FVG Behavior Patterns


```
Gold-specific FVG observations:


1. GOLD FILLS DAILY FVGs ~85% OF THE TIME
   - If there's a daily FVG, expect price to return to it eventually
   - This makes daily FVGs excellent CRT entry zones


2. GOLD RESPECTS THE CE (50%) OF FVGs PRECISELY
   - The midpoint of FVGs on gold is often touched to the dollar
   - Use CE for limit order entries


3. OVERLAPPING FVGs ON GOLD = EXTREME MOMENTUM
   - When gold creates 2-3 FVGs in the same direction on the same timeframe
   - This indicates massive institutional commitment
   - CRT trades in this direction have the highest probability


4. FVGs NEAR ROUND NUMBERS ON GOLD
   - $XX00 and $XX50 levels that coincide with FVGs are 
     the highest confluence zones on gold
   - These are "magnet zones" that pull price with authority


5. NEWS-CREATED FVGs ON GOLD
   - FVGs created by NFP, CPI, FOMC are the largest
   - They take longer to fill but WILL be filled
   - CRT trades that target news FVGs can produce $30-$50+ moves
```


---


 34.6 Element 4: Order Flow Structure (Order Blocks)


 Identifying Key Order Blocks on Gold


```
BULLISH ORDER BLOCK:
- The last bearish candle before a significant up-move
- Represents where institutions placed BUY orders
- Price should respect this zone as support when retested


On XAUUSD:
- Mark the body of the last bearish candle before a rally
- Include the wick if it's significant
- Draw a zone from the open to the low of that candle
- When price returns to this zone → potential CRT entry


BEARISH ORDER BLOCK:
- The last bullish candle before a significant down-move
- Represents where institutions placed SELL orders
- Price should respect this zone as resistance when retested


On XAUUSD:
- Mark the body of the last bullish candle before a drop
- Draw a zone from the open to the high of that candle
- When price returns to this zone → potential CRT entry
```


 Order Block + CRT Confluence


```
THE ULTIMATE CRT ENTRY:


When an order block AND an FVG overlap at the same price zone, 
you have the highest probability entry point possible.


Example on XAUUSD:


Daily bullish order block: $2,340 - $2,348
4H bullish FVG: $2,342 - $2,350
Overlap zone: $2,342 - $2,348


CRT Setup:
- Price sweeps a low (manipulation)
- Displacement creates an MSS
- Price retraces to the overlap zone ($2,342 - $2,348)
- THIS IS THE SNIPER ENTRY
- Stop below the manipulation wick
- Targets based on CRT model


This kind of confluence happens several times per month on gold 
and produces the highest win rates and best R:R trades.
```


---


 34.7 Element 5: Range Structure


 Identifying the Current Dealing Range


```
The DEALING RANGE is the most recent significant swing high to swing low 
that price is trading within.


On XAUUSD Daily:


Step 1: Find the most recent significant swing high
        → Let's say $2,410


Step 2: Find the most recent significant swing low 
        that led to (or resulted from) that high
        → Let's say $2,330


Step 3: This is your dealing range: $2,330 - $2,410


Step 4: Calculate key levels:
        - 50% (Equilibrium): $2,370
        - 75% (Upper Premium): $2,390
        - 25% (Lower Discount): $2,350
        - 62% (OTE Upper): $2,379.60
        - 79% (OTE Upper): $2,393.20


Step 5: Identify your zone:
        - Above $2,370 = PREMIUM (look for shorts)
        - Below $2,370 = DISCOUNT (look for longs)
```


 Range Structure and CRT


```
THE RANGE STRUCTURE RULE:


BULLISH CRT should form in the DISCOUNT zone of the dealing range
→ You're buying cheap


BEARISH CRT should form in the PREMIUM zone of the dealing range
→ You're selling expensive


CRT in the WRONG zone:
- Bullish CRT in premium = lower probability (buying expensive)
- Bearish CRT in discount = lower probability (selling cheap)


CRT at EQUILIBRIUM (50%):
- Can go either way
- Need strong direction and time confirmation
- Not ideal — wait for price to move to premium or discount
```


---


 34.8 The Structure Score Card


```
STRUCTURE SCORE CARD:


Swing Structure:
□ Aligns with trade direction (trending in my direction) → +3
□ Ranging but at range extreme → +1
□ Conflicting with trade direction → -3


Liquidity Structure:
□ Clear unswept liquidity in target direction → +2
□ Manipulation sweep confirmed → +3
□ No clear liquidity to sweep → -1
□ Major liquidity AGAINST my direction → -2


Imbalance Structure (FVGs):
□ Unmitigated FVG at my entry zone → +3
□ Higher TF FVG supports my direction → +2
□ No FVGs in play → -1
□ FVG against my direction at entry → -2


Order Flow Structure:
□ Order Block at my entry zone → +2
□ Order Block + FVG overlap → +3
□ No order block in play → 0
□ Order Block against my direction → -2


Range Structure:
□ Bullish CRT in discount / Bearish CRT in premium → +3
□ At equilibrium → 0
□ Bullish CRT in premium / Bearish CRT in discount → -3


SCORING:
12+ → EXCELLENT STRUCTURE — highest probability
8-11 → GOOD STRUCTURE — proceed normally
4-7 → MODERATE STRUCTURE — reduce size or wait
0-3 → WEAK STRUCTURE — likely skip
Below 0 → POOR STRUCTURE — DO NOT TRADE
```


---


 Chapter 35: The Complete TDS Score — Combining All Three Pillars


 35.1 The Master TDS Score Card


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
         TDS MASTER SCORE CARD
           XAUUSD CRT TRADE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


TRADE: Long / Short ____________
DATE: ____________
TIME: ____________


╔═══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║            PILLAR 1: TIME                 ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                           ║
║ Session:                                  ║
║ □ London KZ (07:00-10:00)     → +3       ║
║ □ NY KZ (13:00-15:00)         → +2       ║
║ □ London-NY overlap           → +2       ║
║ □ Silver Bullet (14:00-15:00) → +1       ║
║ □ Asian session               → -2       ║
║ □ Late NY / After hours       → -1       ║
║                                           ║
║ Day of Week:                              ║
║ □ Tuesday                     → +3       ║
║ □ Wednesday FOMC              → +3       ║
║ □ Wednesday non-FOMC          → +2       ║
║ □ Friday NFP                  → +3       ║
║ □ Thursday                    → +1       ║
║ □ Monday                      → -1       ║
║ □ Friday non-NFP              → -1       ║
║                                           ║
║ Monthly Timing:                           ║
║ □ Week 2-3 (manipulation/distribution) +1 ║
║ □ Week 1 (accumulation)       → 0       ║
║ □ Last 2 days of month        → -1       ║
║                                           ║
║ TIME SCORE: _____ / maximum ~7           ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════╝


╔═══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║          PILLAR 2: DIRECTION              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                           ║
║ Monthly Structure:                        ║
║ □ Supports my direction       → +2       ║
║ □ Against my direction        → -2       ║
║ □ Neutral                     → 0        ║
║                                           ║

║ □ Supports my direction       → +2       ║
║ □ Against my direction        → -2       ║
║ □ Neutral                     → 0        ║
║                                           ║

║ □ Against my direction        → -3       ║

║                                           ║

║                                           ║

║ □ No clear liquidity          → -1       ║
║ □ Liquidity against me        → -2       ║
║                                           ║

║ □ FVG at entry zone           → +3       ║
║ □ HTF FVG supports direction  → +2       ║
║ □ No FVGs                     → -1       ║
║ □ FVG against direction       → -2       ║
║                                           ║
║ Order Flow:                               ║
║ □ OB + FVG overlap at entry   → +3       ║
║ □ OB at entry zone            → +2       ║
║ □ No OB                       → 0        ║
║ □ OB against direction        → -2       ║
║                                           ║
║ Range Position:                           ║
║ □ Correct zone (discount/premium) → +3   ║
║ □ At equilibrium              → 0        ║
║ □ Wrong zone                  → -3       ║
║                                           ║
║ STRUCTURE SCORE: _____ / maximum ~14     ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════╝


═══════════════════════════════════════════════
              TOTAL TDS SCORE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


TIME SCORE:      _____
DIRECTION SCORE: _____
STRUCTURE SCORE: _____
                 ─────
TOTAL:           _____ / maximum ~33


TRADE GRADE:
25+ → A+ GRADE (Take full position, highest confidence)
20-24 → A GRADE (Take full position)
15-19 → B GRADE (Take position, slightly reduced size)
10-14 → C GRADE (Take only if desperate, reduced size)
5-9 → D GRADE (Skip — not worth the risk)
Below 5 → F GRADE (Absolutely do NOT trade)


═══════════════════════════════════════════════
```


 35.2 Using the TDS Score in Practice


Let's walk through a real-world example:


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    EXAMPLE: XAUUSD BULLISH CRT TRADE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


Date: Tuesday
Time: 08:30 GMT (London Killzone)
Trade Direction: LONG


PILLAR 1: TIME
- London KZ: +3
- Tuesday: +3
- Week 2 of month: +1
TIME SCORE: +7 ✓✓✓


PILLAR 2: DIRECTION
- Monthly: Bullish → +2
- Weekly: Bullish → +2
- Daily: Bullish (swept yesterday's low, closing bullish) → +3
- 4H: Just created bullish MSS → +2
- Premium/Discount: Price at $2,345, dealing range 50% at $2,370 
  → in discount → +2
- COT: Institutions net long → +1
DIRECTION SCORE: +12 ✓✓✓


PILLAR 3: STRUCTURE
- Swing Structure: Daily HH/HL pattern → +3
- Liquidity: Just swept PDL and Asian low → +3
- Liquidity targets above: PDH at $2,385, EQH at $2,392 → +2
- Imbalance: 15M FVG at $2,347-$2,352 → +3
- Order Flow: Daily OB at $2,342-$2,348, overlaps with FVG → +3
- Range Position: In discount → +3
STRUCTURE SCORE: +17 (exceeds maximum — multiple confluences)


TOTAL TDS SCORE: 7 + 12 + 17 = 36


TRADE GRADE: A+ ★★★★★


EXECUTION:
- Entry: Limit buy at $2,349.50 (CE of FVG within OB)
- Stop: $2,338 (below manipulation wick at $2,340)
- Risk: $11.50
- T1: $2,360 (daily equilibrium) → close 33%
- T2: $2,385 (PDH) → close 33%
- T3: $2,392 (EQH) → close 34%


This is as good as it gets. Every pillar is fully aligned.
```


---


 Chapter 36: TDS in Different Market Conditions


 36.1 TDS in a Trending Gold Market


```
When XAUUSD is in a strong trend:


TIME:
- Trending markets produce CRT setups daily
- Every London open is a potential CRT entry
- Pullbacks during NY session offer re-entry CRT setups
- Day of week matters less — trends override weekly patterns


DIRECTION:
- All timeframes aligned → strong directional score
- This is the easiest environment for TDS
- Simply trade every CRT in the trend direction and ignore counter-trend setups


STRUCTURE:
- Swing structure is clean (HH/HL or LH/LL)
- Each pullback creates a new FVG for entry
- Order blocks stack in the trend direction
- Premium/Discount works perfectly — buy every dip in discount


STRATEGY:
- Scale in: Use the trend to add to winners
- Each CRT pullback is an addition point
- Trail stops using the last swing point
- Gold trends can produce $50-$100+ moves over 3-5 days
  → Multiple CRT entries during this period can compound gains
```


 36.2 TDS in a Ranging Gold Market


```
When XAUUSD is stuck in a range:


TIME:
- Ranges often form during Asian-heavy periods or between news events
- CRT setups are less frequent in ranges
- When they occur, they're usually at range extremes
- Stick strictly to London KZ — don't force trades


DIRECTION:
- No clear directional bias → Direction score will be low
- In ranges, direction shifts between BUY at range low and SELL at range high
- This is the hardest environment for TDS — be prepared to skip many days


STRUCTURE:
- Swing structure is flat (EQH and EQL)
- Liquidity is pooled at BOTH range extremes
- FVGs from within the range are less reliable
- The KEY: Wait for a sweep of the range extreme → this IS the CRT setup


STRATEGY:
- Only trade CRT at the EDGES of the range
- Sweep of range low → CRT long → target range high
- Sweep of range high → CRT short → target range low
- These are classic "fade the range" setups powered by CRT mechanics
- Reduce position size by 50% in ranging markets
- The eventual range breakout will be the most powerful CRT of all
```


 36.3 TDS During High-Impact News on Gold


```
When XAUUSD faces NFP, CPI, FOMC:


TIME:
- News events override normal session timing
- The killzone shifts to the news release time (13:30 GMT for US data)
- Pre-news: DO NOT trade (accumulation phase)
- During news spike: DO NOT trade (manipulation phase in progress)
- Post-news (15-30 minutes after): PRIME CRT window


DIRECTION:
- Pre-news direction may be IRRELEVANT if news is strong enough
- Post-news direction should be determined by:
  a) Which side was swept during the spike (that's the manipulation)
  b) The displacement direction after the spike (that's the true direction)
  c) Higher timeframe bias as a tiebreaker


STRUCTURE:
- Pre-news structure levels are CRITICAL — they become the liquidity targets
- The news spike will sweep these levels
- Post-news FVGs are the entry zones
- These FVGs are typically very large on gold → use wider stops


STRATEGY:
- Mark the 30-minute consolidation range before news
- Wait for the spike to complete (usually within 5-10 minutes)
- Identify which side of the pre-news range was swept
- Wait for displacement and MSS in the opposite direction
- Enter on the FVG retracement
- Targets: opposite side of pre-news range, then beyond


NEWS CRT SPECIFICS FOR GOLD:
- NFP spike: Usually $10-$25 in the initial direction
- CPI spike: Usually $15-$30 in the initial direction
- FOMC: Can be $20-$50 in the initial direction
- The reversal (CRT true move) is often LARGER than the spike
```


 36.4 TDS During Low Volatility / Compression on Gold


```
When XAUUSD is in a compression / squeeze:


Identification:
- Daily ranges shrinking (ADR below $15-$20)
- Bollinger Bands narrowing (optional visual aid)
- Multiple inside days on the daily chart
- VIX for gold (GVZ) at extremes


TIME:
- Compression can last 2-5 days on gold
- CRT setups during compression are LOW PROBABILITY
- The BREAKOUT from compression creates the BEST CRT setup
- Wait for the compression to break — don't try to trade within it


DIRECTION:
- Impossible to determine during compression itself
- The breakout direction is determined by which side gets swept FIRST
- If compression breaks below (sweeps lows) and reverses → bullish CRT
- If compression breaks above (sweeps highs) and reverses → bearish CRT


STRUCTURE:
- Compression creates EQH and EQL at the compression boundaries
- MASSIVE liquidity pools at both edges
- Whoever sweeps this liquidity first = manipulation side
- The resulting move can be $30-$60+ as all the compressed energy releases


STRATEGY:
- Mark the compression range meticulously
- Set alerts at both boundaries
- When one boundary is broken, switch to execution timeframe (5M/1M)
- Wait for displacement and MSS
- Enter CRT with tight stop (just beyond the compression wick)
- Target is minimum the full compression range in the opposite direction
- Often targets 2-3x the compression range
```


---


 Chapter 37: TDS-CRT Daily Workflow — The Complete System


 37.1 The Morning Analysis Protocol (Pre-London)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
   TDS-CRT MORNING ANALYSIS — XAUUSD
          (Complete before 07:00 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


STEP 1: TIME ASSESSMENT (5 minutes)
─────────────────────────────────────
□ What day of the week is it?
  → Monday/Friday = lower priority
  → Tuesday = highest priority
  → Note day score


□ Any high-impact news today?
  → Check economic calendar
  → Note time of release
  → If major news: adjust killzone to news time


□ Where are we in the month?
  → Week 1/2/3/4?
  → Month-end adjustments needed?


□ TIME VERDICT: _______ (Trade / Caution / No Trade)


STEP 2: DIRECTION ASSESSMENT (10 minutes)
─────────────────────────────────────
□ Monthly chart check:
  → Structure: HH/HL or LH/LL?
  → Key monthly levels?
  → Monthly direction: ____________


□ Weekly chart check:
  → How did last week's candle close?
  → Weekly FVGs / OBs?
  → Weekly direction: ____________


□ Daily chart check:
  → Yesterday's candle analysis
  → Daily structure
  → Where is price in the daily dealing range?
  → Daily direction: ____________


□ 4H chart check:
  → Last 2-3 4H candle analysis
  → 4H FVGs and OBs
  → 4H direction: ____________


□ DIRECTION VERDICT: _______ (Bullish / Bearish / Unclear)


STEP 3: STRUCTURE ASSESSMENT (10 minutes)
─────────────────────────────────────
□ Mark on chart:
  → PDH: _______
  → PDL: _______
  → Asian High: _______ (mark after Asian close at ~07:00)
  → Asian Low: _______
  → EQH: _______
  → EQL: _______
  → Round Numbers: _______
  → Daily/4H FVGs: _______
  → Daily/4H OBs: _______
  → Dealing Range 50%: _______
  → Premium Zone: Above _______
  → Discount Zone: Below _______


□ Identify:
  → Primary liquidity target (most likely to be swept): _______
  → Secondary liquidity target: _______
  → Expected CRT manipulation level: _______
  → Expected CRT entry zone: _______


□ STRUCTURE VERDICT: _______ (Supportive / Neutral / Conflicting)


STEP 4: GENERATE TDS SCORE
─────────────────────────────────────
□ TIME Score: _______
□ DIRECTION Score: _______
□ STRUCTURE Score: _______
□ TOTAL TDS Score: _______
□ TRADE GRADE: _______


STEP 5: CREATE THE TRADE PLAN
─────────────────────────────────────
"Today I am looking for a [LONG/SHORT] CRT setup."
"My manipulation target is [LEVEL] to be swept."
"My entry zone is [FVG/OB between X and Y]."
"My stop loss will be at [LEVEL] ($____ risk)."
"My lot size will be [X lots] (1% risk)."
"My targets are:"
  "T1: _______ (close 33%)"
  "T2: _______ (close 33%)"
  "T3: _______ (close 34%)"
"I will only take this trade if MSS is confirmed."
"I will not trade if TDS score is below 15."


STEP 6: SET ALERTS AND WAIT
─────────────────────────────────────
□ Alert set at manipulation target level
□ Alert set at killzone start time
□ Chart marked with all levels
□ Position calculator prepared
□ Walk away until alert triggers


═══════════════════════════════════════════════
```


 37.2 The Execution Protocol (During Killzone)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    TDS-CRT EXECUTION — XAUUSD
       (During Active Killzone)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


ALERT TRIGGERED: Price approaching manipulation level


STEP 1: CONFIRM TIME
□ Am I in a killzone? → YES/NO
□ If NO → Do not trade, reset


STEP 2: CONFIRM DIRECTION
□ Has anything changed since morning analysis? → YES/NO
□ If YES → Re-assess direction, may need to abort


STEP 3: WATCH THE MANIPULATION
□ Is price sweeping the marked level?
□ Is it a clean sweep (wick through, immediate rejection)?
□ Switch to execution timeframe (15M/5M)


STEP 4: WAIT FOR DISPLACEMENT
□ Is there a large candle against the sweep direction?
□ Is it creating an FVG?
□ If NO displacement within 15-20 minutes → may not be a CRT, stand down


STEP 5: CONFIRM MSS
□ Has the most recent lower TF swing point been broken?
□ Is it a clean break (not just a wick)?
□ If NO MSS → DO NOT ENTER, wait


STEP 6: EXECUTE
□ All confirmations received
□ Place limit order at FVG (CE level)
□ Set stop loss beyond manipulation wick
□ Set T1 with OCO order if possible
□ Set T2 and T3 as manual alerts


STEP 7: MANAGE
□ T1 hit → close 33%, SL to breakeven
□ T2 hit → close 33%, trail SL
□ T3 hit → close remaining
□ Time-based exit if targets not reached within expected window


═══════════════════════════════════════════════
```


 37.3 The Post-Trade Protocol


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    TDS-CRT POST-TRADE — XAUUSD
═══════════════════════════════════════════════


STEP 1: RECORD THE TRADE
□ Fill in complete trade journal (CRT + TDS scoring)
□ Screenshot the chart at entry, during, and at exit
□ Note the TDS score for this trade


STEP 2: ASSESS TDS ACCURACY
□ Was the TIME correct? (Did the setup form when expected?)
□ Was the DIRECTION correct? (Did price move as expected?)
□ Was the STRUCTURE correct? (Did levels hold as expected?)
□ Which pillar was strongest? Weakest?


STEP 3: EMOTIONAL ASSESSMENT
□ Did I follow the process exactly?
□ Did I feel any urge to deviate from the plan?
□ Did I experience FOMO, revenge, or overconfidence?
□ Rate my emotional state: 1 (poor) to 10 (excellent)


STEP 4: TDS LEARNING
□ What would I do differently next time?
□ Did any TDS factor surprise me?
□ Should I adjust my TDS scoring weights?
□ Add notes to my TDS reference sheet


STEP 5: PREPARE FOR NEXT SETUP
□ Are conditions right for another trade today?
□ If 2 losses already → STOP for the day
□ If daily risk limit hit → STOP for the day
□ If conditions are fresh → repeat morning analysis for next KZ


═══════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 Chapter 38: TDS Edge Cases and Special Situations


 38.1 When TDS Pillars Conflict


Sometimes the three pillars don't agree. Here's how to handle each conflict:


 Time is Good, Direction is Good, Structure is Bad


```
Scenario: It's Tuesday at London open (TIME ✓), all timeframes 
are bullish (DIRECTION ✓), but price is in the premium zone 
and there's no FVG or OB at the current price (STRUCTURE ✗)


Decision: SKIP THE TRADE


Why: Even perfect time and direction cannot overcome 
poor structural positioning. You'd be buying at a bad price.


Alternative: Wait for price to pull back to discount zone 
where structure supports the trade. The CRT manipulation 
may be the pullback that takes price to your structural entry.
```


 Time is Good, Structure is Good, Direction is Unclear


```
Scenario: It's London killzone (TIME ✓), price is at a clean 
FVG + OB in discount (STRUCTURE ✓), but weekly is bearish 
while daily is bullish (DIRECTION unclear)


Decision: TRADE WITH REDUCED SIZE (50%)


Why: Structure and Time are objective. Direction conflicts 
can resolve quickly. But uncertainty warrants caution.


Alternative: Wait for the 4H to resolve the direction conflict 
(4H MSS in one direction confirms) before entering full size.
```


 Direction is Good, Structure is Good, Time is Bad


```
Scenario: Daily and Weekly are strongly bearish (DIRECTION ✓), 
price just swept a high and has a perfect FVG entry (STRUCTURE ✓), 
but it's 04:00 GMT during Asian session (TIME ✗)


Decision: SET AN ALERT AND WAIT


Why: The setup may be valid, but executing during Asian session 
on gold often leads to stop hunts before the real move.


Alternative: Place a limit order at the FVG BUT only if it would 
be filled during London killzone. If it fills during Asian, 
the trade may be premature. Use a time-based filter: 
"Order only active after 07:00 GMT."
```


 38.2 The "Perfect Storm" — When All TDS Pillars Align Perfectly


```
This happens approximately 2-4 times per month on XAUUSD.


Characteristics:
- TIME: Tuesday or Wednesday in London KZ during Week 2 of the month
- DIRECTION: All four timeframes (M, W, D, 4H) agree
- STRUCTURE: Liquidity sweep at a premium/discount level with 
  FVG + OB overlap and clean swing structure


What to do:
- This is your bread and butter trade
- Take FULL position (1% risk)
- Consider even 1.5% risk if your track record supports it
- Hold for extended targets (T3 and beyond)
- This is the type of trade that makes your month


Performance expectation:
- Win rate on Perfect Storm setups: 70-80%
- Average R:R: 1:3 to 1:5
- These trades alone, taken 2-4x per month, can produce consistent profitability
```


 38.3 "TDS Override" Situations


Sometimes, extraordinary circumstances override normal TDS analysis:


```
Override 1: GEOPOLITICAL SHOCK
- War, sanctions, central bank emergency action
- TDS analysis becomes temporarily irrelevant
- Price may move $50-$100 in hours
- Strategy: Stand aside until volatility normalizes, 
  then look for the CRT that forms after the shock


Override 2: FLASH CRASH / LIQUIDITY VACUUM
- Gold drops $30+ in minutes with no fundamental catalyst
- Usually caused by algorithmic cascade
- This IS a CRT on the macro level — massive SSL sweep
- Wait for displacement and MSS on the 1H, then enter CRT long
- Some of the best CRT trades in history form after flash crashes


Override 3: SYSTEMATIC RISK EVENT
- 2008-style financial crisis, COVID crash, etc.
- Gold can either spike (safe haven) or crash (margin calls)
- Normal TDS may not apply for days or weeks
- Switch to Weekly/Monthly TDS only
- Don't try to scalp CRT during systemic events
```

 Chapter 39: TDS Performance Metrics


 39.1 Tracking TDS Effectiveness


Beyond standard trade metrics, track these TDS-specific metrics:


```
TDS METRIC TRACKING SHEET:


Monthly Statistics:
─────────────────


TRADE GRADES:
- A+ trades taken: _____ | Win rate: _____% | Avg R: _____
- A trades taken: _____ | Win rate: _____% | Avg R: _____
- B trades taken: _____ | Win rate: _____% | Avg R: _____
- C trades taken: _____ | Win rate: _____% | Avg R: _____
- D/F trades taken: _____ | Win rate: _____% | Avg R: _____


(Your A+ trades should have the highest win rate. 
If C trades have a higher win rate than A trades, 
your scoring system needs recalibration.)


PILLAR ACCURACY:
- TIME accuracy (trade formed when expected): _____% 
- DIRECTION accuracy (market moved in expected direction): _____%
- STRUCTURE accuracy (levels held as expected): _____%


(All three should be above 60%. If any is below 50%, 
review how you're assessing that pillar.)


SESSION PERFORMANCE:
- London KZ trades: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____ 
- NY KZ trades: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____
- Other times: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____


(London KZ should be your best performer on gold.)


DAY OF WEEK PERFORMANCE:
- Monday: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____
- Tuesday: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____
- Wednesday: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____
- Thursday: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____
- Friday: Win rate _____% | Avg R _____


(Use this data to refine your day-of-week scoring.)
```


 39.2 Optimizing TDS Over Time


After 3+ months of data:


```
OPTIMIZATION PROCESS:


1. REVIEW GRADE PERFORMANCE
   - If A+ trades underperform A trades → your "perfect" criteria may be too strict or include a factor that actually hurts performance
   - If B trades perform nearly as well as A → you can be less selective (take more B-grade trades)
   - If C trades lose money consistently → STOP taking C-grade trades entirely


2. REVIEW PILLAR WEIGHTS
   - If TIME is your strongest predictor → increase TIME weight in scoring
   - If STRUCTURE matters more than DIRECTION → adjust weights
   - Personalize the scorecard based on YOUR data


3. REVIEW XAUUSD-SPECIFIC FACTORS
   - Which liquidity levels get swept most reliably?
   - Which FVG timeframes produce the best entries?
   - What stop size has the optimal balance of tight + safe?
   - Which targets are consistently reached?


4. CREATE YOUR PERSONAL "EDGE PROFILE"
   After enough data, you'll discover YOUR specific edge:
   - "I perform best on Tuesday London KZ bullish CRT trades 
     in discount zones with 15M FVG entries and $8 stops 
     targeting PDH" (example)
   - This becomes your SIGNATURE TRADE
   - Focus 80% of your trading on your signature trade
```

 Chapter 40: TDS Quick Reference — The Complete Integration


 40.1 The TDS-CRT One-Page Master Cheat Sheet


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
         TDS-CRT MASTER SYSTEM — XAUUSD
              COMPLETE QUICK REFERENCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


PRE-TRADE: TDS FILTER
──────────────────────


T — TIME
    ✓ London KZ (08:00-10:00) or NY KZ (13:00-15:00)
    ✓ Tuesday, Wednesday (FOMC), or Friday (NFP)
    ✓ Week 2-3 of month
    ✗ Asian session, Monday, late NY, month-end


D — DIRECTION
    ✓ Weekly + Daily + 4H all agree
    ✓ Price in correct zone (discount for longs, premium for shorts)
    ✓ COT supports direction
    ✗ Conflicting timeframes, at equilibrium, counter-trend


S — STRUCTURE
    ✓ Clear swing structure (HH/HL or LH/LL)
    ✓ Identified liquidity to be swept
    ✓ FVG and/or OB at expected entry zone
    ✓ In correct premium/discount zone
    ✗ No clear levels, conflicting structure, no FVG/OB


TDS SCORE: Minimum 15 to trade, 25+ for full size


──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


TRADE: CRT EXECUTION
─────────────────────


1. MANIPULATION → Level swept? (PDH/PDL, Asian H/L, EQH/EQL)
2. DISPLACEMENT → Large opposite candle? FVG created?
3. MSS → Lower TF swing point broken?
4. ENTRY → Limit at FVG CE (or market at MSS)
5. STOP → Beyond manipulation wick + spread buffer
6. TARGETS:
   T1: Parent candle open (33% close, SL to BE)
   T2: Opposite range side (33% close, trail SL)
   T3: Next liquidity pool (close all)


──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


POST-TRADE: REVIEW
───────────────────


□ Journal: TDS score, entry, result, emotional state
□ TDS accuracy: Which pillar was strongest/weakest?
□ Process: Did I follow every step?
□ Learning: One improvement for next trade


──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


RISK RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
□ 1% maximum risk per trade
□ 2% maximum daily risk
□ 5% maximum weekly risk
□ 2 consecutive losses = stop for session
□ Minimum 1:2 R:R
□ Only trade during killzones
□ MSS required before every entry


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 40.2 Final Integration: The TDS-CRT Trader's Creed


```
I am a TDS-CRT trader.


I do not trade randomly.
I trade with TIME alignment — knowing when the market 
is most likely to deliver my setup.


I do not guess direction.
I read DIRECTION from structure — letting the market 
tell me which way it wants to go.


I do not enter blindly.
I confirm STRUCTURE — ensuring that price is in the 
right location, at the right levels, with the right 
institutional footprints.


Only when TIME, DIRECTION, and STRUCTURE align 
do I look for my CRT setup.


And when the CRT forms — 
when liquidity is swept, 
when displacement confirms, 
when market structure shifts — 


I execute with precision, 
manage with discipline, 
and accept the outcome with grace.


This is my edge.
This is my system.
This is how I trade XAUUSD.
```
