 CHAPTER 12: TIME-OF-DAY PATTERNS IN XAUUSD


 The Hour-by-Hour Intelligence Guide


---


 12.1 — THE XAUUSD HOURLY PATTERN PROFILE


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   XAUUSD HOUR-BY-HOUR BEHAVIORAL PROFILE (ALL TIMES GMT)


   00:00 – 02:00 (Asian Early):
   → Very low volume
   → Wide spreads
   → Often continues previous day's closing direction
   → Low CRT quality
   → Action: Monitor only — no trades unless exceptional


   02:00 – 05:00 (Asian Mid):
   → Shanghai Gold Exchange active (SGE)
   → Chinese institutional order flow
   → Can create significant directional moves if
     Chinese domestic news is significant
   → Moderate CRT quality for Asian specialist setups
   → Action: Take only very high TDS setups (9+)


   05:00 – 07:00 (Pre-London):
   → European institutional activity begins warming up
   → XAUUSD begins showing increased directionality
   → Pre-London stop positioning visible
   → Asian range becoming defined
   → Action: Mark Asian range high and low.
             Monitor for final Asian consolidation.
             No new entries (too close to London open volatility).


   07:00 – 08:30 (London Open):
   → CRITICAL WINDOW — Highest quality CRT opportunity
   → London open sweeps the Asian session range
   → Spread tightens to minimum
   → Institutional order flow surges
   → CRT patterns of the Asian range high or low
     frequently form in this window
   → Action: MAXIMUM ATTENTION. Best setups of the day
             often form here.


   08:30 – 10:00 (London Morning):
   → Post-open trend develops
   → Quality CRT setups continue
   → Second opportunity after London open sweep
   → Silver Bullet window begins (09:00)
   → Action: Full execution protocol. Prime session.


   10:00 – 11:00 (Silver Bullet Window):
   → ICT Silver Bullet Protocol active
   → 10:30 AM: LBMA AM Fix — potential CRT around fix
   → High-quality FVG setups frequently form
   → Action: Silver Bullet protocol in addition to CRT.
             Watch 10:30 fix time specifically.


   11:00 – 12:00 (London Mid):
   → Volume moderating from morning surge
   → Continuation of morning's established direction
   → Lower quality CRT setups (consolidation common)
   → Action: Take only very clean setups. Increase TDS +1.


   12:00 – 13:00 (Pre-NY):
   → Quiet period — London winding down, NY not yet active
   → Spread can widen slightly
   → Low volume — avoid new positions
   → Action: Prepare for NY open. Review existing positions.


   13:00 – 14:30 (NY Open):
   → CRITICAL WINDOW — Second major CRT opportunity
   → NY open sweep of London session extremes common
   → COMEX futures open → volume surge
   → Institutional repositioning between London and NY
   → Action: MAXIMUM ATTENTION. NY open CRT setup.


   14:00 – 16:00 (NY Silver Bullet Window):
   → ICT NY Silver Bullet Protocol active
   → 15:00 GMT: LBMA PM Fix — potential CRT opportunity
   → Action: Silver Bullet protocol.
             Watch 15:00 PM Fix specifically.


   16:00 – 17:00 (NY Mid-Session):
   → NY session body — moderate activity
   → London close at 17:00 → volume shift
   → Action: Standard protocol. Moderate quality.


   17:00 – 20:00 (Post-London):
   → Only US institutions active
   → Volume drops
   → Lower liquidity → wider spreads
   → Lower CRT quality
   → Action: Reduce size by 25%. Only high TDS setups.


   20:00 – 22:00 (NY Close):
   → Very low volume
   → Widening spreads
   → Avoid new positions
   → Close any intraday positions taken without swing plan
   → Action: Monitor only. No new entries.


   22:00 – 00:00 (Rollover):
   → Broker rollover time — swap charges applied
   → Potential spike from institutional rollover activity
   → Spreads widen significantly
   → Action: NO TRADING. Mandatory no-trade window.


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 CHAPTER 13: MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION


 Reading the Market's Current Personality


---


 13.1 — THE FOUR MARKET REGIMES


Gold markets cycle through distinct behavioral regimes.


Each regime requires a modified approach to the CRT system.


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   THE FOUR XAUUSD MARKET REGIMES


   REGIME 1: TRENDING (BULL OR BEAR)
   ────────────────────────────────────
   Identification:
   → Daily chart shows clear higher highs + higher lows
     (bull) or lower lows + lower highs (bear)
   → H4 structure confirms the same direction
   → Moving averages aligned (if used for reference)
   → ATR is moderate to elevated
   
   Behavioral Characteristics:
   → CRT pullbacks are shallow (bull) or shallow pops (bear)
   → CRT sweeps are quickly reversed and continue trend
   → Targets are more easily achieved
   → Counter-trend setups have high failure rate
   
   System Adjustment:
   → Strongly favor trend-direction CRT setups
   → Extend targets (trend momentum carries trades further)
   → Use trailing stops to capture extended moves
   → Counter-trend setups require TDS minimum + 2
   → Increase T3 (runner) allocation to 50% of position


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   REGIME 2: RANGING (DEFINED BOUNDARIES)
   ────────────────────────────────────────
   Identification:
   → Price oscillating between clear support and resistance
   → No confirmed BOS on H4 in either direction
   → ATR declining or below average
   → Equal or near-equal highs and lows forming
   
   Behavioral Characteristics:
   → CRT sweeps at range boundaries are highly reliable
   → CRT setups in the middle of the range are unreliable
   → Mean-reversion is the dominant behavior
   → Breakout setups fail frequently (fake breakouts)
   
   System Adjustment:
   → Take CRT setups only at range extremes (not middle)
   → T1 = 50% of range, T2 = opposite boundary
   → Do not use extended targets (price unlikely to break)
   → Reduce position size at range midpoint entries
   → When range DOES break: Apply trending regime rules


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   REGIME 3: VOLATILE (IMPULSIVE, UNDEFINED)
   ──────────────────────────────────────────
   Identification:
   → ATR 2× or more above 30-day average
   → Large, impulsive candles in multiple directions
   → Structure forming and breaking rapidly
   → Often associated with major news events
   
   Behavioral Characteristics:
   → CRT patterns form but fail more frequently
   → Manipulation sweeps are larger and more violent
   → Price behavior is less predictable
   → High slippage on stops
   
   System Adjustment:
   → Reduce position size by 40–50%
   → Widen stops by 50% beyond normal
   → Increase TDS minimum by 2 points
   → Reduce to maximum 1 trade per day
   → Consider not trading until regime normalizes
   → If trading: Set and forget approach (no active management
     due to screen-time risk in volatile conditions)


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   REGIME 4: COMPRESSING (PRE-BREAKOUT)
   ──────────────────────────────────────
   Identification:
   → Progressively smaller daily ranges
   → ATR declining for 5+ consecutive sessions
   → Price forming converging pattern (triangle/wedge)
   → Equal highs AND equal lows forming simultaneously
   
   Behavioral Characteristics:
   → False breakouts common (liquidity collection on both sides)
   → The eventual breakout is often explosive
   → The first breakout direction is sometimes reversed
     (the false breakout sweep) before the real move
   
   System Adjustment:
   → Reduce position size by 25% (difficult to predict direction)
   → Do not trade inside the compression zone
   → Wait for the breakout + CRT confirmation
   → The post-breakout CRT is often the best setup
     of an entire week or month
   → When the breakout occurs: Apply trending regime rules
     immediately and aggressively


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 CHAPTER 14: BUILDING THE COMPLETE MARKET INTELLIGENCE DASHBOARD


 Synthesizing All Intelligence Into One Decision Framework


---


 14.1 — THE MASTER MARKET INTELLIGENCE DASHBOARD


The market intelligence dashboard synthesizes all intelligence layers into a single pre-session reference.


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
╔═════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║     XAUUSD MASTER MARKET INTELLIGENCE DASHBOARD     ║
║              Daily Pre-Session Review               ║
╚═════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝


DATE: _______________ | SESSION: _________________


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SECTION 1: MACRO CONTEXT
──────────────────────────
Fed Policy Phase:
□ Rate Hike Cycle → Bearish Bias
□ Peak Rate Pause → Transitional / Bullish developing
□ Rate Cut Cycle → Bullish Bias
□ Emergency Easing → Volatile then Bullish


Current Real Yield Environment:
□ Negative and falling → Strongly Bullish
□ Negative but rising → Neutral/Mild Bearish
□ Positive and rising → Bearish
□ Positive but falling → Neutral/Mild Bullish


COT Positioning Summary (from Friday report):
Large Specs: Net Long / Net Short / Extreme Long / Extreme Short
Commercial Hedgers: Net Long / Net Short
Small Specs: Net Long / Net Short


COT Signal: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 2: CORRELATION ANALYSIS
─────────────────────────────────
DXY Direction: ↑ Rising / ↓ Falling / → Flat
DXY Signal: Bearish Gold / Bullish Gold / Neutral


US10Y Yield Direction: ↑ Rising / ↓ Falling / → Flat
Yield Signal: Bearish Gold / Bullish Gold / Neutral


XAGUSD Alignment: Confirming Gold Direction / Diverging
SPX500 (Regime): Risk-Off (Bullish Gold) / Risk-On / Neutral


Combined Correlation Signal:
□ STRONGLY BULLISH (3+ bullish signals, 0 bearish)
□ BULLISH (2 bullish, 0-1 bearish)
□ NEUTRAL (mixed signals)
□ BEARISH (2 bearish, 0-1 bullish)
□ STRONGLY BEARISH (3+ bearish, 0 bullish)


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 3: SEASONAL CONTEXT
─────────────────────────────
Month: _______________ | Seasonal Bias: ___________
Week of Month: 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 | Event Week: Yes / No
Day of Week: _______________ | Day Quality: H/M/L


Seasonal Signal: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 4: MARKET REGIME
──────────────────────────
Current Regime: Trending Bull / Trending Bear /
                Ranging / Volatile / Compressing


Regime Signal:
□ Trending → Strongly prefer trend direction
□ Ranging → Only trade at extremes
□ Volatile → Reduce size 40-50%
□ Compressing → Wait for breakout


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 5: GEOPOLITICAL CONTEXT
─────────────────────────────────
Active Geopolitical Risks: None / Minor / Moderate / Major
Risk Classification: Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3 / None
Geopolitical Signal: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 6: ECONOMIC CALENDAR TODAY
─────────────────────────────────────
Event 1: _________ Time: _____ Impact: T1/T2/T3/T4
Event 2: _________ Time: _____ Impact: T1/T2/T3/T4
Event 3: _________ Time: _____ Impact: T1/T2/T3/T4


Calendar Action Required:
□ No action (T3/T4 only)
□ Close by [time] (T2 event)
□ Full news protocol (T1 event)


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 7: TECHNICAL CONTEXT
──────────────────────────────
Daily Bias: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral
H4 Bias: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral
Combined Technical Bias: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral


Key Levels Today:
Resistance 1: ___________ | Source: ___________
Resistance 2: ___________ | Source: ___________
Support 1: ______________ | Source: ___________
Support 2: ______________ | Source: ___________


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


SECTION 8: COMPOSITE INTELLIGENCE SIGNAL
──────────────────────────────────────────
Macro Context: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral (+1/0/-1)
Correlation: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral (+1/0/-1)
Seasonal: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral (+1/0/-1)
Regime: Trend Bull / Trend Bear / Other (+1/0/-1)
Geopolitical: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral (+1/0/-1)
Technical: Bullish / Bearish / Neutral (+1/0/-1)


COMPOSITE SCORE: +___ / 6 (bullish signals count)
                 -___ / 6 (bearish signals count)


FINAL COMPOSITE SIGNAL:
Score +4 to +6: STRONGLY BULLISH
  → Take only long CRT setups today
  → Standard TDS applies to longs
  → Short setups require TDS minimum + 2


Score +2 to +3: BULLISH
  → Prefer long CRT setups
  → Short setups require TDS minimum + 1


Score -1 to +1: NEUTRAL
  → No directional preference
  → Standard TDS applies in both directions
  → Follow technical analysis only


Score -2 to -3: BEARISH
  → Prefer short CRT setups
  → Long setups require TDS minimum + 1


Score -4 to -6: STRONGLY BEARISH
  → Take only short CRT setups today
  → Standard TDS applies to shorts
  → Long setups require TDS minimum + 2


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TODAY'S COMPOSITE SIGNAL: ___________________________
PREFERRED DIRECTION: ________________________________
TDS ADJUSTMENT: ____________________________________
SIZE ADJUSTMENT: ___________________________________
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 CHAPTER 15: INTEGRATING MARKET UNDERSTANDING WITH THE CRT SYSTEM


 Putting the Complete Intelligence Layer to Work


---


 15.1 — THE INTEGRATION HIERARCHY


All intelligence gathered in Part 9 integrates with the trading system through a clear hierarchy:


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   THE COMPLETE INTEGRATION HIERARCHY


   LEVEL 1 — MACRO INTELLIGENCE (Months to quarters):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   → Fed policy cycle position
   → Real yield environment
   → Central bank gold demand trend
   → De-dollarization megatrend
   
   Function: Sets the LONG-TERM BIAS
   How used: When macro is strongly bullish →
             Hold winning long trades longer.
             When macro is bearish → Cut losses faster
             on longs. Be more patient with short runners.


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   LEVEL 2 — CORRELATION ANALYSIS (Days to weeks):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   → DXY direction
   → US10Y yield direction
   → XAGUSD alignment
   → Broad market regime
   
   Function: Sets the MEDIUM-TERM BIAS
   How used: Daily correlation dashboard informs
             the session's preferred direction.
             Adjusts TDS requirements as described.


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   LEVEL 3 — SEASONAL CONTEXT (Weeks to months):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   → Monthly seasonal bias
   → Weekly intra-month patterns
   → Day of week patterns
   
   Function: Adds PROBABILISTIC CONTEXT to setups
   How used: Seasonal alignment adds confidence.
             Seasonal conflict requires higher TDS.


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   LEVEL 4 — ECONOMIC CALENDAR (Day-specific):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   → Today's events and their impact tier
   → Pre-event and post-event protocols
   
   Function: CONTROLS RISK around scheduled events
   How used: Strict timing protocols around events.
             Post-event CRT opportunities identified.


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   LEVEL 5 — MARKET REGIME (Session-specific):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   → Current trending/ranging/volatile/compressing state
   
   Function: MODIFIES SYSTEM PARAMETERS
   How used: Trending → extend targets.
             Ranging → take first target aggressively.
             Volatile → reduce size.
             Compressing → wait for breakout.


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   LEVEL 6 — TECHNICAL ANALYSIS (Trade-specific):
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   → CRT pattern identification
   → TDS scoring
   → ICT model alignment
   → SMC structure context
   
   Function: IDENTIFIES THE SPECIFIC ENTRY
   How used: This is where all the intelligence
             above is ACTED UPON.
             The CRT pattern is the trigger.
             All other intelligence informs whether
             to take it, how large to size it,
             and how aggressively to manage it.


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

---


 15.3 — PART 9 CORE PRINCIPLES SUMMARY


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
╔═════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║        PART 9 — CORE PRINCIPLES SUMMARY             ║
╚═════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝


PRINCIPLE 1:
XAUUSD microstructure is the mechanical foundation
of the CRT pattern. The sweep is not random — it is
the institutional collection of retail stop liquidity.
Understanding the mechanism makes the pattern
more trustworthy and more consistently executable.


PRINCIPLE 2:
The gold market operates across multiple layers:
physical market, futures market (COMEX), OTC spot
(XAUUSD), and ETF market. Each layer influences
the spot price in specific ways that can be
anticipated and incorporated into the HTF bias.


PRINCIPLE 3:
DXY is the primary real-time driver of XAUUSD.
Every trade session begins with a DXY analysis.
When DXY and XAUUSD are aligned — trade with
confidence. When they diverge — require additional
TDS confirmation before entering.


PRINCIPLE 4:
Real yields (nominal yields minus inflation
expectations) are the most important macro
driver of medium-term gold trends. Negative
and falling real yields = strongest gold bull signal.
Positive and rising real yields = strongest bear signal.


PRINCIPLE 5:
The Federal Reserve policy cycle creates the most
predictable multi-month gold trends. The transition
from peak rates to rate cuts is the highest-opportunity
period for long XAUUSD positions.


PRINCIPLE 6:
News events are not obstacles — they are opportunity
generators. The post-news CRT pattern is among the
most powerful setups in the system. The protocol is:
protect during the event, exploit the pattern after.


PRINCIPLE 7:
Seasonal patterns in gold are driven by physical
demand cycles (Indian/Chinese jewelry and gift buying),
institutional calendar effects, and economic event
clustering. August through October is the seasonally
strongest bullish window. April through May is the
weakest. Seasonal context modifies — but never
replaces — technical and macro analysis.


PRINCIPLE 8:
Day-of-week patterns matter. Tuesday is the highest
quality CRT day of the week. Friday afternoon is
the lowest quality. The first Monday of the month
(NFP preview) and NFP Friday are the bookends of
the monthly institutional cycle.


PRINCIPLE 9:
Market regime determines how the system parameters
are applied. Trending → extend targets. Ranging →
take first targets aggressively. Volatile → reduce
size. Compressing → wait for the breakout CRT.
Reading the regime correctly is as important as
reading the individual setup.


PRINCIPLE 10:
The Market Intelligence Dashboard synthesizes all
intelligence into a composite directional signal.
This signal modifies the TDS threshold requirements
and creates a directional preference for the session.
It does not override the CRT pattern — it informs
how aggressively to execute it.


PRINCIPLE 11:
The complete intelligence hierarchy operates from
macro (months) through correlations (weeks) through
seasonal (months) through calendar (days) through
regime (sessions) to technical (trades). Each layer
feeds the one below it. The trade is the smallest
unit — supported by the entire intelligence structure.


PRINCIPLE 12:
Gold is not just a chart. It is the intersection of
global monetary policy, physical demand cycles,
geopolitical risk, currency dynamics, and institutional
positioning. The trader who understands these forces
does not just react to price — they anticipate
the conditions that make specific CRT patterns
high-probability and trade them with deep conviction.


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

---


 SECTION 1: HOW TO USE THE SETUP LIBRARY


 The Architecture of the Encyclopedia


---


 1.1 — THE PURPOSE OF THE SETUP LIBRARY


The Setup Library is the most practical reference document in this entire system.


Every setup documented in this library represents a specific, repeatable market pattern that has been observed, cataloged, and refined through the framework of the CRT, TDS, SMC, and ICT methodologies applied specifically to XAUUSD.


The purpose of the library is threefold:


Purpose 1 — Pattern Recognition Training:
By studying each setup in detail — complete with its identification criteria, entry rules, stop placement, and target logic — the trader builds a comprehensive visual and conceptual library that accelerates real-time pattern recognition during live trading sessions.


Purpose 2 — Execution Reference:
During live trading, when a setup begins forming, the trader references the relevant setup entry in this library to confirm that all criteria are being correctly identified and that the execution parameters are appropriate.


Purpose 3 — Backtesting Framework:
Each setup in this library has specific, defined criteria. These criteria form the exact rules that are applied during the backtesting process described in Part 7 — ensuring that backtesting is testing what is actually traded in live markets.


---


 1.2 — THE SETUP TEMPLATE


Every setup in this library follows an identical template structure.


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   UNIVERSAL SETUP TEMPLATE


   SETUP : [Number]
   SETUP NAME: [Full descriptive name]
   CATEGORY: [CRT / ICT / SMC / Session / News / Advanced]
   DIFFICULTY: [Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced]
   FREQUENCY: [Daily / Weekly / Monthly]
   EXPECTED WIN RATE: [Approximate % based on testing]
   MINIMUM R:R: [Minimum acceptable ratio]
   OPTIMAL SESSION: [Asian / London / New York / Any]


   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


   SETUP OVERVIEW:
   [2–4 sentence description of what this setup is
   and why it works mechanically]


   MARKET CONDITIONS REQUIRED:
   [What macro and regime conditions make this
   setup most reliable]


   IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
   [Step-by-step criteria that must ALL be present
   for this to qualify as a valid setup]


   TDS MINIMUM SCORE: [Minimum TDS required]
   TDS ELEVATED SCORE: [Score that makes this A+ quality]


   ENTRY RULES:
   [Exact entry methodology — price, trigger, timing]


   STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
   [Exact structural level + buffer instructions]


   TARGET STRUCTURE:
   T1: [First target logic]
   T2: [Second target logic]
   T3: [Extended target logic]


   TRADE MANAGEMENT:
   [Specific management rules for this setup type]


   COMMON ERRORS:
   [The most frequent mistakes traders make with this setup]


   DISTINGUISHING FROM SIMILAR SETUPS:
   [How to tell this apart from lookalike patterns]


   QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
   A+ Setup: [Criteria for highest quality version]
   A Setup:  [Standard quality criteria]
   B Setup:  [Marginal quality — consider skipping]


   XAUUSD SPECIFIC NOTES:
   [Any gold-specific characteristics that affect
   this setup's behavior]


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 SECTION 2: CRT SETUP CATALOG


 Setups 1 Through 15


---


 SETUP 1 — THE CLASSIC LONDON OPEN CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 1: THE CLASSIC LONDON OPEN CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 3–5 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 48–55%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London Open (07:00–08:30 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The Classic London Open CRT is the highest-frequency
and highest-reliability setup in the entire system.
It occurs when London session participants sweep the
Asian session's accumulated stop orders — above the
Asian high (bearish CRT) or below the Asian low
(bullish CRT) — and then reverse sharply as
institutional orders are filled at the swept level.


MARKET CONDITIONS REQUIRED:
→ Asian session must have formed a clear range
  (not a trend — a consolidation with defined extremes)
→ HTF (H4/Daily) bias should support the CRT direction
→ DXY should be aligned (DXY falling = gold bullish CRT)
→ No tier-1 news within 60 minutes of setup formation


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Asian session has formed with clear high and low
  (minimum 3 hours of consolidation visible)
□ London session opens (07:00 GMT)
□ Price moves decisively toward either Asian extreme
  (the sweep leg — should be at least 100 pips minimum)
□ Price sweeps BEYOND the Asian high or low by
  minimum 50 pips
□ Price begins returning into the Asian session range
  within the first H1 candle of London
□ The H1 candle that created the sweep closes BACK
  inside the Asian range (this is the CRT candle)
□ TDS score calculated: MINIMUM 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary Entry: Market order at the open of the H1
candle following the CRT candle close
Secondary Entry: Limit order at 50% of the CRT candle
body (for better R:R on the retracement)


Trigger confirmation:
→ CRT candle closes INSIDE the Asian range
→ Close must be in the UPPER half of the CRT candle
  for bullish setup (lower half for bearish)


TDS MINIMUM SCORE: 7
TDS ELEVATED SCORE: 9 (A+ quality)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Structural level: The CRT candle extreme
  (the sweep wick tip — the lowest/highest point)
→ Buffer: Add 80–120 pips beyond the CRT extreme
→ Liquidity check: Is there another obvious level
  beyond the CRT extreme? If yes, place stop beyond it.
→ Minimum stop distance: 150 pips (London session ATR-based)


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33% of position): Previous Asian session midpoint
                       OR 1:1 R (whichever comes first)
T2 (33% of position): Opposite Asian session extreme
                       (the Asian high for bullish CRT,
                        the Asian low for bearish CRT)
T3 (33% runner):      Next significant H4 level
                       OR 1:3 R (whichever is structural)


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ Move stop to breakeven when T1 is reached
→ Partial (T2) at the Asian opposite extreme
→ Trail the runner using structure trail method
→ Close the entire position before 12:00 GMT
  if T2 has not been reached (London session fading)


COMMON ERRORS:
ERROR 1: Entering DURING the sweep (before CRT candle closes)
→ The CRT candle has not completed — pattern may extend
→ ALWAYS wait for the candle to close


ERROR 2: Ignoring the Asian range quality
→ A messy, directionless Asian session produces
  a lower-quality CRT target
→ The Asian range must be clean with clear extremes


ERROR 3: Trading counter to strong H4 trend
→ When H4 is in a strong downtrend, the bullish
  London CRT will frequently fail
→ TDS minimum must be increased to 9 when CRT
  is counter to the H4 trend


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, clear Asian range, strong DXY alignment,
          HTF bias aligned, sweep exceeds 100 pips,
          clean CRT candle close well inside Asian range


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, clear Asian range, DXY roughly aligned,
          sweep of 50–100 pips, CRT candle closes inside range


B Setup:  TDS 6–7, Asian range somewhat defined,
          DXY neutral, sweep of 30–50 pips
          Consider skipping or reducing size by 50%


XAUUSD SPECIFIC NOTES:
→ XAUUSD Asian ranges are typically 300–600 pips
→ The sweep extension is usually 80–200 pips beyond
  the Asian extreme in standard conditions
→ On high-volatility days (ATR > 2,000): Sweep can
  be 300–500 pips — widen stop accordingly
→ Tuesday and Thursday produce the cleanest
  London CRT setups statistically


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 2 — THE BULLISH NY OPEN CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 2: THE BULLISH NY OPEN CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 45–52%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: NY Open (13:00–14:30 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The NY Open CRT exploits the institutional repositioning
that occurs as New York participants take over from
London. Price frequently sweeps the London session low
(for bullish CRT) before reversing and trading higher
through the NY session. The sweep collects sell stops
from traders who went short during the London session
and enables institutional long position accumulation.


MARKET CONDITIONS REQUIRED:
→ London session must have established a clear range
  with defined high and low
→ The London session should show some directional bias
  (not a perfectly flat range)
→ DXY at or approaching resistance (supporting gold)
→ No FOMC or NFP within 2 hours


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ London session (07:00–13:00 GMT) formed clear range
□ NY open (13:00 GMT) price action begins
□ Price drops toward and through the London session low
□ Sweep extends minimum 50–100 pips below the London low
□ Price reverses and begins climbing back
□ First H1 candle after the sweep closes ABOVE the
  London session low (confirming the sweep was a fake-out)
□ Volume (if available): Should show spike at sweep low
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order on the second H1 candle
         open (after the sweep CRT candle closes)
Alternative: Limit order at the 50% level of the
             NY open H1 sweep candle body


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the NY open sweep wick extreme
→ Buffer: 100–150 pips below the wick
→ Minimum stop: 200 pips (NY session volatility)


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): London session midpoint (halfway point
          of the London range)
T2 (33%): London session high (the opposite extreme)
T3 (33%): Next H4 resistance level or 1:3 R


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ This setup has a time constraint:
  The NY-driven move typically completes by 17:00 GMT
→ If T2 not reached by 16:30 GMT → close remainder
→ Do not hold through London close (17:00 GMT)
  without moving stop to T1 minimum


COMMON ERRORS:
ERROR 1: Confusing NY continuation with NY reversal
→ When London was bullish AND NY sweeps the London
  low → this is a bullish CRT (NY continuation long)
→ When London was bearish AND NY sweeps the London
  low → this may be a false reversal (cautious)
→ HTF bias must support the direction


ERROR 2: Entering too early during the sweep
→ The sweep may extend 200+ pips below the London low
→ Entering during the sweep can result in a 200-pip
  adverse move before the CRT candle closes


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, DXY clearly rejecting resistance,
          H4 bias bullish, London low clearly swept
          with momentum reversal, time within Silver
          Bullet window (14:00–16:00 GMT overlap)


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, DXY neutral to falling, H4 bias
          supportive, clean sweep and reversal


B Setup:  TDS 6, DXY rising (USD strengthening),
          H4 bias neutral
          Consider reducing size to 50%


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 3 — THE BEARISH NY OPEN CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 3: THE BEARISH NY OPEN CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 44–51%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: NY Open (13:00–14:30 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
Mirror image of Setup 2. The NY Open Bearish CRT
occurs when price sweeps the London session HIGH at
the NY open — collecting buy stop orders from bullish
London traders and breakout buyers — before reversing
sharply lower as institutional short positions are
accumulated at the swept high.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ London session formed clear range with defined high
□ NY open: Price rallies toward and through London high
□ Sweep extends minimum 50 pips above London high
□ First H1 candle after the sweep closes BELOW the
  London session high (inside the London range)
□ DXY: Rising or rejecting support (USD strengthening)
□ H4 bias: Bearish or transitioning to bearish
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order on candle open after CRT
         candle closes below London high
Stop: Above the NY open sweep wick + 100–150 pip buffer
T1: London session midpoint
T2: London session low
T3: Next H4 support or 1:3 R


COMMON ERRORS:
→ Taking this setup when H4 is strongly bullish
  (counter-trend setups require TDS 9+)
→ Not waiting for the CRT candle to close inside
  the London range before entering


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, DXY clearly at support and bouncing,
          H4 showing bearish transition, London high
          swept cleanly with reversal conviction


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, DXY neutral to rising, H4 neutral
B Setup:  TDS 6, DXY falling → increase TDS requirement


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 4 — THE ASIAN SESSION RANGE CRT (BULLISH)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 4: THE ASIAN SESSION RANGE CRT (BULLISH)
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 38–45%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: Asian (02:00–07:00 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
This setup occurs during the Asian session itself —
not at the London open. A CRT pattern forms within
the Asian session range, sweeping a micro-level low
and reversing. This is a lower-frequency, lower-quality
version of the London CRT because Asian liquidity is
thinner. It is only traded when the TDS score is elevated.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Clear Asian session structure on H1
□ A prior swing low is visible within the Asian session
□ Price sweeps below that swing low (minimum 50 pips)
□ The sweep candle closes back ABOVE the swept low
□ Shanghai Gold Exchange is active (02:00–07:00 GMT)
□ HTF (H4/Daily) bias is bullish
□ DXY is falling or rejecting resistance
□ TDS minimum: 9 (higher requirement — lower quality session)


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Limit order at 50% of the CRT candle body
         (better fill in low-volume Asian conditions)
Alternative: Market order on the following candle open


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the CRT candle wick extreme
→ Buffer: 150–200 pips (Asian sessions can re-test levels)
→ Minimum stop: 200 pips


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (50%): Previous Asian swing high (within session)
T2 (50%): Asian session high or first H4 resistance


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ This is primarily a scalp-oriented setup
→ Close the entire position before London open
  (07:00 GMT) to avoid London open reversal risk
→ OR: Reduce to 25% position and trail into London


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 10, H4 strongly bullish, DXY clearly falling,
          Shanghai Exchange active, sweep clearly defined


B Setup:  TDS 8 → consider skipping. Asian session
          CRTs have lower reliability. Only trade at 9+.


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 5 — THE DAILY HIGH/LOW CRT SWEEP


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 5: THE DAILY HIGH/LOW CRT SWEEP
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 46–54%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
This setup targets the sweep of the PREVIOUS DAY'S
high or low — one of the most reliable liquidity
pools in XAUUSD. The previous day's high accumulates
buy stops (from breakout traders) and stop losses
(from short traders). When swept and reversed, this
creates a high-probability CRT entry.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify yesterday's high and low on the H1 chart
□ Mark both levels clearly
□ During London or NY session: Price approaches and
  sweeps beyond one of these levels by minimum 50 pips
□ The sweep candle (H1) closes BACK inside the previous
  day's range
□ The sweep is decisive — not a slow grind through
□ HTF bias supports the direction of the reversal
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order on the next H1 candle open
Alternative: Limit order at the CRT candle's 61.8%
             retracement level (Fibonacci-based entry)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Beyond the swept wick extreme
→ Buffer: 80–100 pips
→ Check: Is there another obvious liquidity level
  beyond the stop? If yes, move stop beyond that level.


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): Previous day's midpoint (50% level of yesterday's range)
T2 (33%): Opposite extreme of the previous day
           (if swept the high → target is the previous day's low)
T3 (33%): Next significant H4 structural level


COMMON ERRORS:
→ Missing the setup because the previous day's levels
  were not pre-marked on the chart
→ Solution: Mark previous day's high and low at the
  START of every new session — before any trading begins


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, sweep of BOTH previous day's level
          AND a psychological round number simultaneously,
          DXY aligned, H4 bias aligned


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, clean sweep of previous day level,
          HTF bias supporting, DXY confirming


B Setup:  TDS 6, previous day level swept but HTF neutral
          → Skip or reduce to 50% size


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 6 — THE WEEKLY HIGH/LOW CRT SWEEP


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 6: THE WEEKLY HIGH/LOW CRT SWEEP
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per month
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–58%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The weekly high or low represents one of the most
significant liquidity pools in XAUUSD. When swept
during an active session, the reversal CRT is
typically of higher quality and larger magnitude
than daily or session-based CRTs. These setups are
less frequent but more powerful.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Previous week's high or low clearly identified
□ Current week: Price approaches and sweeps beyond
  the previous week's extreme by minimum 80–150 pips
□ The sweep occurs during an active session (not overnight)
□ A clear H1 CRT pattern forms with the sweep wick
□ Volume spike visible at the swept level (if data available)
□ H4 and Daily bias aligned with reversal direction
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order on the next H1 candle after
         the CRT candle closes inside the weekly level
         (the CRT candle close must be clearly inside
          the previous week's range)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Beyond the swept wick extreme
→ Buffer: 120–180 pips
→ This is a larger setup — stops are wider
→ Position size adjusted proportionally (same dollar risk)


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (25%): 1:1 R (secure initial profit)
T2 (25%): Previous week's midpoint (50% of weekly range)
T3 (25%): Opposite weekly extreme (high/low)
T4 (25%): Runner — trail using H4 structure


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ This is a multi-session setup
→ After T1 and T2 taken → trail using session-based
  trailing stop method (from Part 5)
→ Hold the runner until the week closes or
  trailing stop is triggered


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 10, weekly level + psychological round
          number coincide, H4 AND Daily bias aligned,
          DXY strongly aligned, sweep is decisive
          (large wick, strong rejection candle)


A Setup:  TDS 8–9, clean weekly level sweep, H4 aligned,
          Daily neutral to supporting


B Setup:  TDS 7, weekly level swept but only H4 aligned
          → Reduce to swing portion only (smaller size)


XAUUSD SPECIFIC NOTES:
→ XAUUSD weekly sweeps tend to occur on:
  Monday (testing previous Friday's close levels)
  Thursday (pre-NFP week positioning)
  Friday (end-of-week position squaring)
→ The best weekly CRTs align with the monthly
  seasonal bias discussed in Part 9


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 7 — THE EQUAL HIGHS CRT (DOUBLE TOP SWEEP)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 7: THE EQUAL HIGHS CRT (DOUBLE TOP SWEEP)
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–3 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 47–55%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
Equal highs on the H1 chart represent one of the most
concentrated retail liquidity pools available. Every
retail trader who learned technical analysis has
been taught that equal highs represent "strong
resistance." They place sell orders at this level
and stop losses just above it. Institutional
participants target this concentrated stop cluster —
sweeping it to collect liquidity before reversing.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Two or more H1 candle highs within 20–30 pips of each other
□ These equal highs should be separated by at minimum
  3 candles (not adjacent candles)
□ Price approaches these equal highs from below
□ Price sweeps above both equal highs by minimum 40 pips
□ The sweep candle closes back BELOW the equal highs level
□ The sweep is decisive — a single candle with a clear wick
  (not a slow grind above the level)
□ HTF bias: Should be bearish or neutral-to-bearish
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order short on the next candle after
         the sweep candle closes back below equal highs
Alternative: Sell limit order placed at the equal
             highs level (anticipating the sweep before
             the CRT candle closes)
             Note: Limit orders here require higher TDS (9+)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Above the sweep wick extreme
→ Buffer: 60–80 pips above the wick
→ Minimum stop: 150 pips


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): 1:1 R or next significant support level
T2 (33%): Major H1 support below (swing low)
T3 (33%): H4 support level or 1:3 R


COMMON ERRORS:
ERROR 1: Trading equal highs against a strong uptrend
→ When H4 is strongly bullish, equal highs frequently
  break through rather than holding
→ This setup requires at minimum H4 neutral bias


ERROR 2: Only looking for perfect "equal" highs
→ Equal highs do not need to be pixel-perfect
→ Within 30 pips is close enough for significant
  liquidity accumulation


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, three or more equal highs (triple top
          level), H4 bearish or at significant H4 resistance,
          DXY at support and bouncing, sweep is sharp
          and decisive with strong bearish close


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, two clear equal highs, H4 neutral
          to bearish, DXY neutral


B Setup:  TDS 6, two roughly equal highs, H4 bullish
          → Skip (counter-trend risk too high)


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 8 — THE EQUAL LOWS CRT (DOUBLE BOTTOM SWEEP)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 8: THE EQUAL LOWS CRT (DOUBLE BOTTOM SWEEP)
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–3 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 47–55%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The bullish mirror image of Setup 7. Equal lows
on the H1 chart represent concentrated sell stop
and stop loss liquidity for long traders. The
institutional sweep below the equal lows collects
this liquidity before a bullish reversal.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Two or more H1 candle lows within 20–30 pips of each other
□ Separated by minimum 3 candles between the equal lows
□ Price approaches these equal lows from above
□ Price sweeps below both equal lows by minimum 40 pips
□ The sweep candle closes back ABOVE the equal lows level
□ The rejection from the sweep low is decisive
□ HTF bias: Should be bullish or neutral-to-bullish
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order long on the next candle after
         the sweep candle closes above the equal lows
Alternative: Buy limit at the equal lows level
             (anticipatory entry — requires TDS 9+)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the sweep wick extreme
→ Buffer: 60–80 pips below the wick
→ Minimum stop: 150 pips


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): 1:1 R or next significant resistance
T2 (33%): Major H1 resistance above
T3 (33%): H4 resistance or 1:3 R


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, triple equal lows, H4 bullish or at
          H4 support, DXY at resistance and falling,
          sharp spike below with immediate bullish close


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, two clear equal lows, H4 neutral
          to bullish, DXY confirming


B Setup:  TDS 6, equal lows visible but H4 bearish
          → Skip (counter-trend)


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 9 — THE ROUND NUMBER CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 9: THE ROUND NUMBER CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Beginner-Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 3–5 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 44–52%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: Any active session
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
Psychological round numbers in XAUUSD (1900, 1950,
2000, 2050, 2100, etc.) accumulate massive quantities
of stop orders and limit orders. Every $50 and $100
increment is a significant liquidity level. The CRT
sweep of these levels — followed by a reversal — is
one of the most consistent and tradeable patterns
in the entire gold market.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify the nearest $50 increment round number
  to current price
□ Price is approaching that round number
□ Price sweeps beyond the round number by minimum 30–50 pips
□ A clear CRT candle forms: Sweeps the round number,
  then closes back on the other side of it
  (e.g., sweeps above 2,000 then closes below 2,000)
□ The sweep is decisive — wick clearly beyond the number
□ TDS minimum: 6 (round number CRTs are slightly more
  reliable at lower TDS due to the concentration of
  liquidity at these specific levels)


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order on the next H1 candle after
         the CRT candle closes back through the round number
Alternative: Limit order at the round number itself
             (anticipating the sweep and return)
             Note: This is a more aggressive entry —
             requires TDS 8+ to justify


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Beyond the sweep wick extreme
→ Buffer: 50–80 pips
→ The stop must be positioned so that IF another
  more significant round number is nearby (within 100 pips),
  the stop goes BEYOND that second round number as well


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): Previous significant H1 level
          OR 1:1 R (whichever comes first)
T2 (33%): Next structural level in the reversal direction
T3 (33%): 1:3 R or the next major round number
          in the reversal direction


COMMON ERRORS:
ERROR 1: Underestimating the sweep extension at major
         round numbers ($2,000, $1,500, $1,000)
→ Major round numbers can be swept by 150–300 pips
→ Use ATR-based stops (wider than minor round numbers)


ERROR 2: Not checking if a round number is near a
         significant structural level (OB, FVG, H4 level)
→ When a round number AND a structural level coincide:
  This is an A+ setup
→ The combined liquidity concentration makes the
  sweep more likely and the reversal more powerful


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: Round number coincides with H4 OB or FVG,
          TDS 9+, DXY aligned, session is London or NY open,
          sweep is 80+ pips with clear rejection candle


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, clean round number sweep, DXY
          moderately aligned, active session


B Setup:  TDS 5–6, round number swept but during
          low-volume period (Asian late session),
          → Reduce size by 50%


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 10 — THE MULTI-SESSION CRT SWEEP


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 10: THE MULTI-SESSION CRT SWEEP
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–58%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London Open
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The Multi-Session CRT occurs when a single sweep
simultaneously takes out the highs or lows of
MULTIPLE previous sessions — the previous Asian high
AND the previous day's high, for example. This multi-
level sweep creates a particularly powerful reversal
because it has simultaneously cleared the stop
clusters from multiple timeframes and multiple
trader populations.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify 2 or more session levels that are close
  together (within 50–100 pips of each other):
  Example: Previous day's high = 2,010.00
           Previous Asian session high = 2,008.50
□ Price sweeps ABOVE both levels simultaneously
  (a single wick that takes out both)
□ The sweep candle closes back below BOTH levels
□ This creates a combined liquidity grab of
  extraordinary power
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Same as Classic London CRT (1) but with the
understanding that the target can be significantly
larger (multiple session extremes means more
liquidity collected = larger institutional position)


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (25%): 1:1 R
T2 (25%): Previous session low (the mirror level)
T3 (25%): Previous day's low (the full reversal)
T4 (25%): Runner — trail to capture extended move


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: Three levels swept simultaneously (Asian +
          Daily + Round number), TDS 10, strong DXY
          alignment, clear reversal candle pattern


A Setup:  Two levels swept, TDS 8–9, HTF aligned


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 11 — THE FIBONACCI CRT CONFLUENCE


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 11: THE FIBONACCI CRT CONFLUENCE
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 2–3 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–58%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The Fibonacci CRT Confluence occurs when a CRT sweep
aligns with a significant Fibonacci retracement level
from a larger market structure move. The 61.8% and
78.6% retracement levels, in particular, accumulate
both Fibonacci-based orders AND the normal stop
clusters that create CRT sweeps. The combined
liquidity makes this setup particularly reliable.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify a significant swing on H4 or Daily
  (the leg that was the most recent directional move)
□ Apply Fibonacci retracement to that swing
□ Note the 61.8% and 78.6% retracement levels
□ On H1: A CRT sweep forms at or within 30 pips
  of one of these Fibonacci levels
□ The CRT sweep and the Fibonacci level coincide
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order after CRT candle close confirms
         both the Fibonacci level AND the CRT pattern
         (both must be visible — not one or the other)


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Beyond the 100% Fibonacci retracement level
→ Buffer: 50–80 pips beyond the 100% level
→ Reasoning: If price trades beyond the 100% level,
  the swing that created the Fibonacci is fully retraced
  and the trade thesis is invalid


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): 38.2% Fibonacci extension (modest target)
T2 (33%): Previous swing high/low (structural target)
T3 (33%): 127.2% or 161.8% extension (extended target)


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: CRT sweep at EXACTLY the 78.6% Fibonacci level,
          TDS 10, DXY aligned, AND the level coincides
          with an H4 OB or key structural level


A Setup:  CRT sweep at 61.8% or 78.6%, TDS 8–9,
          H4 bias aligned


B Setup:  CRT sweep near (within 50 pips but not exact)
          a Fibonacci level, TDS 7
          → Reduce size — the confluence is weaker


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 12 — THE FAILED BREAKOUT CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 12: THE FAILED BREAKOUT CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–3 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–60%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The Failed Breakout CRT (also called the "Fakeout")
occurs when price appears to break out of a significant
consolidation or resistance level — triggering breakout
buyers or sellers — and then immediately reverses
back inside the range. This is a textbook CRT
manipulation where breakout traders become the
liquidity for institutional positioning.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ A clear consolidation range or resistance level
  visible on H1 (minimum 4 hours of formation)
□ Price breaks above resistance or below support
  in what appears to be a genuine breakout
  (minimum 50–80 pip break beyond the level)
□ Breakout buyers/sellers enter (the liquidity)
□ Within 1–3 H1 candles: Price reverses back INSIDE
  the range (the failed breakout is confirmed)
□ The candle that re-enters the range closes inside it
  (this is the CRT candle)
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order short (if failed upside breakout)
         or long (if failed downside breakout) on
         the candle after re-entry into the range
Stop: Above the failed breakout wick (for short entries)
      Below the failed breakout wick (for long entries)


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): Opposite boundary of the consolidation range
T2 (33%): Next significant structural level
T3 (33%): 1:3 R or major H4 structure


COMMON ERRORS:
→ Entering too early before the "failed" part is confirmed
→ The breakout may be genuine — wait for price to
  re-enter the range with conviction before entering


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: Failed breakout at a major weekly or
          monthly level, TDS 9+, strong volume divergence
          at the breakout (if volume data available),
          sharp and immediate reversal back inside range


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, clear consolidation range,
          clean failed breakout and re-entry


B Setup:  TDS 6, range somewhat defined,
          re-entry is slow (not sharp)
          → Reduce size


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 13 — THE HIGH-IMPACT NEWS CRT


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 13: THE HIGH-IMPACT NEWS CRT
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per month (tied to news calendar)
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 52–62%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: Post-News (Any)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The High-Impact News CRT is the post-news setup
described in detail in Part 9, Chapter 7. A high-
impact news event creates a sharp initial move —
sweeping stop orders on one or both sides —
followed by a reversal that creates a classic CRT
pattern on the H1 chart. The news event itself
IS the CRT manipulation candle.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Tier-1 economic event has just fired (NFP, CPI, FOMC)
□ Wait minimum 10 minutes after the event
□ Price has made a decisive initial move (500+ pips)
□ The first H1 candle after the news spike shows
  rejection from the extreme (long wick back toward
  the pre-news range)
□ The second H1 candle (the CRT candle) closes
  toward the reversal direction
□ Spread has normalized (below 100 pips)
□ TDS minimum: 9 (elevated — news setups are complex)
□ Size reduction: 75% of normal


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order after the reversal is confirmed
         on the H1 (the CRT candle has closed showing
         the reversal direction)
Entry window: 15–45 minutes after the news event


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Beyond the news spike extreme
→ Buffer: 150–200 pips
→ This is a wider stop than normal due to
  residual news volatility


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (50%): Pre-news range boundary (return to pre-news levels)
T2 (50%): Key structural level in the reversal direction


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: The news outcome is decisive (large beat or miss),
          the reversal is sharp and clean, TDS 10,
          the HTF bias clearly supported the reversal
          direction before the news even fired


A Setup:  TDS 8–9, clean reversal pattern, 15 minutes
          after news, spread normalized


XAUUSD SPECIFIC NOTES:
→ NFP and CPI post-news CRTs have historically
  produced the largest single-session R results
→ The combination of high volatility + institutional
  repositioning creates ideal CRT conditions
→ Never attempt this setup during the first 5 minutes
  post-release — spreads are still extreme


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 14 — THE CRT WITHIN AN HTF IMBALANCE


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 14: THE CRT WITHIN AN HTF IMBALANCE
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 52–60%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
This setup occurs when an H1 CRT pattern forms
INSIDE a significant H4 or Daily FVG (imbalance zone).
The H4 FVG provides institutional support or resistance
that amplifies the probability of the H1 CRT reversal.
The two timeframes working together create a powerful
confluent setup.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify an unmitigated H4 or Daily FVG
  (a gap in price structure that has not been filled)
□ Price enters that H4/Daily FVG zone
□ WITHIN the FVG zone: An H1 CRT pattern forms
  (price creates a sweep and reversal inside the FVG)
□ The CRT sweep occurs DEEPER into the FVG
  (not at the FVG boundary — inside it)
□ The CRT reversal direction aligns with the expected
  reaction from the FVG (FVG is meant to be filled
  from below → bullish CRT inside it is the entry)
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Same as standard CRT entry after the CRT candle closes
The position is now confirmed by BOTH the CRT pattern
AND the H4/Daily FVG zone providing institutional backing


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): Top/Bottom of the H4/Daily FVG
T2 (33%): Previous structural level beyond the FVG
T3 (33%): Next H4 structural level or 1:3 R


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: CRT forms at the DEEPEST point inside the FVG
          (maximum imbalance zone), TDS 10, Daily FVG
          (not just H4), strong DXY alignment


A Setup:  CRT inside H4 FVG, TDS 8–9, HTF bias confirmed


B Setup:  CRT at the FVG boundary (not inside it)
          → This is a weaker version — reduce size


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 15 — THE CONTINUATION CRT (TREND REENTRY)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 15: THE CONTINUATION CRT (TREND REENTRY)
CATEGORY: CRT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week (in trending markets)
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–60%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:1.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
In a trending market, price does not move in a straight
line. It trends → retraces → trends again. The
Continuation CRT is the pullback entry within an
established trend. Price retraces to collect stop orders
from early trend traders (a CRT sweep of their stops)
before continuing in the trend direction.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Clear H4 uptrend (higher highs + higher lows) or
  downtrend (lower lows + lower highs) established
□ Price is in a pullback/retracement phase
  (counter-trend move within the larger trend)
□ The retracement reaches a key level:
  → 38.2%, 50%, or 61.8% Fibonacci of the last trend leg
  → OR a previous H4 support (now resistance) level
  → OR an H4 OB from the original trend push
□ At this retracement level: An H1 CRT pattern forms
  (sweep of a nearby swing low in an uptrend,
   sweep of a nearby swing high in a downtrend)
□ The CRT sweep is in the OPPOSITE direction of the trend
  (a brief dip lower in an uptrend to collect stops)
□ The CRT reversal is back in the TREND direction
□ TDS minimum: 7


ENTRY RULES:
Primary: Market order after CRT candle closes
         confirming the reversal toward trend direction


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the CRT sweep extreme (for long entries in uptrend)
→ Buffer: 80–120 pips
→ Note: In strong trends, this stop should be wide
  enough to survive the next wave of trend momentum


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (25%): Previous swing high in the trend
T2 (25%): 1.272 Fibonacci extension of the retracement
T3 (25%): 1.618 Fibonacci extension (measured move)
T4 (25%): Runner — trail using structure in trend direction


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: TDS 9+, retracement to 61.8% Fibonacci,
          H4 OB present at the retracement level,
          Daily AND H4 trend direction aligned,
          CRT sweep is sharp and decisive


A Setup:  TDS 7–8, retracement to 38.2–50% Fibonacci,
          H4 trend confirmed


B Setup:  TDS 6, shallow retracement (less than 38.2%),
          H4 trend weakening
          → Skip — potential trend exhaustion


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SECTION 3: ICT MODEL SETUP CATALOG


 Setups 16 Through 25


---


 SETUP 16 — ICT LONDON SILVER BULLET (BULLISH)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 16: ICT LONDON SILVER BULLET (BULLISH)
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 42–50%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London (09:00–11:00 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The ICT Silver Bullet is a specific ICT-defined entry
model that uses a Fair Value Gap (FVG) created during
a specific 2-hour window as the entry mechanism.
The London Silver Bullet window is 09:00–11:00 GMT.
Within this window, price creates an FVG during a
directional move, then returns to fill that FVG,
providing the entry point. The entry is long when
the HTF bias is bullish.


MARKET CONDITIONS REQUIRED:
→ H4 and Daily bias BULLISH
→ DXY falling or rejecting resistance
→ No tier-1 news within the 09:00–11:00 window
→ Not the day before or after FOMC/NFP


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Time is between 09:00 and 11:00 GMT (strictly)
□ Price makes an initial bullish move during the window
□ That initial move creates a visible Bullish FVG on H1:
  → A 3-candle pattern where the first candle high
    does not overlap with the third candle low
  → The gap between them is the FVG
□ Price retraces back into the FVG zone
□ Entry trigger: Price enters the FVG zone


ENTRY RULES:
Limit order: Placed at the top of the FVG
             (where price first enters the zone)
Alternative: Market order when price is inside the FVG


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the FVG low (the bottom of the gap)
→ Buffer: 40–60 pips below the FVG low
→ If the FVG low coincides with a swing low: Place
  stop below THAT swing low + buffer


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (50%): 1:1 R
T2 (50%): Session high or next significant resistance


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ The Silver Bullet has a time constraint:
  If T1 is not reached by 11:00 GMT → reassess
→ After 11:00 GMT: Apply trailing stop to any open position
  (the Silver Bullet window has closed)


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: FVG created in the first 30 minutes of window,
          FVG size is significant (100+ pips), HTF strongly
          bullish, DXY clearly falling, CRT pattern on
          H1 confirms the bullish direction simultaneously


A Setup:  FVG visible, TDS 7+, HTF bullish, DXY neutral


B Setup:  Small FVG (less than 50 pips), TDS 6
          → Reduce size


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 SETUP 17 — ICT LONDON SILVER BULLET (BEARISH)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 17: ICT LONDON SILVER BULLET (BEARISH)
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 42–50%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London (09:00–11:00 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
Mirror image of Setup 16. A Bearish FVG is created
during the London Silver Bullet window (09:00–11:00 GMT),
and price returns to fill the gap from below — providing
the short entry signal. Requires H4/Daily bearish bias
and DXY support or rising.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Time: 09:00–11:00 GMT strictly
□ H4 and Daily: BEARISH bias confirmed
□ Price makes initial bearish move → creates Bearish FVG
□ Price retraces upward into the FVG zone
□ Entry: At the bottom of the FVG zone (as price fills it)


ENTRY RULES:
Sell limit at the bottom of the FVG (as price enters)
Stop: Above the FVG high + 40–60 pip buffer
T1 (50%): 1:1 R or session low
T2 (50%): Next significant support or H4 structure


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: Large FVG (100+ pips), created in first 30 min,
          H4 strongly bearish, DXY rising or at support,
          accompanied by CRT on H1 confirming direction

B Setup:  Small FVG, TDS 6, H4 neutral → Reduce size

 SETUP 18 — ICT NEW YORK SILVER BULLET (BULLISH)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 18: ICT NEW YORK SILVER BULLET (BULLISH)
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Intermediate
FREQUENCY: 2–4 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 40–48%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: NY (14:00–16:00 GMT)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The New York Silver Bullet mirrors the London version
but operates in the 14:00–16:00 GMT window (2:00–4:00 PM
EST). The higher volatility of the NY session can
create larger FVGs but also requires wider stops.
The 3:00 PM LBMA PM Fix at 15:00 GMT falls within
this window — often creating the FVG opportunity.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Time: 14:00–16:00 GMT strictly
□ H4/Daily: Bullish bias
□ Initial bullish impulse creates a visible Bullish FVG
□ Price retraces into the FVG zone
□ PM Fix time (15:00 GMT): Check for fix-related FVG


ENTRY RULES:
Same as London Silver Bullet 16
Adjustment: Wider stops due to NY session volatility
            Add 20–40 pips to the stop distance


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (50%): 1:1 R
T2 (50%): Session high or close of NY session


TRADE MANAGEMENT:
→ NY Silver Bullet: Close by 17:00 GMT (London close)
  if targets not reached
→ Can hold through NY close if T1 taken and stop at BE


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
Same structure as London Silver Bullet with adjustment:
→ NY Silver Bullets have slightly lower win rate due
  to increased volatility and more complex market dynamics
→ Require TDS 8+ minimum (vs 7 for London)


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 SETUP 19 — ICT IFVG BULLISH ENTRY


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 19: ICT IFVG BULLISH ENTRY
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 46–54%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The Inversion Fair Value Gap (IFVG) occurs when a
bearish FVG is created (price moves down leaving a gap),
but price then trades through it — inverting the FVG
from bearish to bullish. When price subsequently returns
to the IFVG from above, it should now act as support
rather than resistance. The entry is on the return
to the IFVG zone from above.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ A Bearish FVG is created on H1 (3-candle pattern
  moving down with a visible gap)
□ Price subsequently trades THROUGH the entire Bearish FVG
  (price trades below the FVG low) — this inverts it
□ Price then retraces back UP into the inverted FVG zone
□ Within the IFVG zone: An H1 CRT pattern optionally
  forms (enhances the setup significantly)
□ HTF bias: BULLISH on H4/Daily
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Limit order: At the top of the IFVG zone
             (where price first enters from below)
Alternative: Market order inside the IFVG zone


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the IFVG low (the bottom of the inverted gap)
→ Buffer: 50–70 pips below the IFVG low
→ The IFVG low is the invalidation point —
  if price goes below it, the inversion has failed


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): Top of the original FVG range (the gap extremes)
T2 (33%): Next significant H1/H4 resistance
T3 (33%): 1:3 R or H4 structural target


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: IFVG created during London session, TDS 10,
          H4 strongly bullish, CRT pattern visible
          within the IFVG zone, DXY falling


A Setup:  TDS 8–9, clear IFVG formation, H4 bullish
B Setup:  TDS 7, IFVG not perfectly formed → Skip


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```


---


 SETUP 20 — ICT IFVG BEARISH ENTRY


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 20: ICT IFVG BEARISH ENTRY
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 46–54%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The bearish IFVG mirror. A Bullish FVG is created but
price trades back through it (below the FVG low),
inverting it from bullish to bearish. When price then
returns to the IFVG from below, it should act as
resistance — providing the short entry.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ A Bullish FVG is created on H1
□ Price trades through the entire Bullish FVG (above FVG high)
□ Price retraces back DOWN into the inverted FVG zone
□ HTF bias: BEARISH on H4/Daily
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Sell limit at the bottom of the IFVG zone
Stop: Above the IFVG high + 50–70 pip buffer
T1 (33%): Bottom of the original FVG range
T2 (33%): Next H1/H4 support
T3 (33%): 1:3 R


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
Same structure as IFVG Bullish (19) — same quality
criteria applied in reverse for bearish direction


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

 SETUP 21 — ICT UNICORN MODEL (BULLISH)


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 21: ICT UNICORN MODEL (BULLISH)
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–58%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2.5 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The ICT Unicorn Model is a high-precision entry that
requires the convergence of three ICT elements: a Break
of Structure (BOS), an Order Block (OB), and a Fair Value
Gap (FVG) — all aligned in the same zone. When all three
align, the entry point is within the OB/FVG overlap zone.
This is one of the highest-precision setups in the system
and produces some of the most reliable entries.


MARKET CONDITIONS REQUIRED:
→ H4/Daily bullish bias (for bullish Unicorn)
→ Active session (London or NY — not Asian)
→ No tier-1 news within 1 hour


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ A clear BOS has occurred on H1:
  → Price breaks above a significant swing high
  → This is the market shift confirmation
□ Price retraces back toward the BOS origin
□ Identify the Order Block:
  → The last BEARISH H1 candle BEFORE the bullish BOS move
  → This candle's body is the OB zone
□ Identify the FVG:
  → Within the bullish BOS move, find the 3-candle FVG
□ The OB and FVG must OVERLAP:
  → Part of the OB zone must be inside the FVG zone
  → This overlap zone is the entry zone
□ Price retraces into this overlap zone
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Limit order: At the upper boundary of the OB/FVG overlap zone
             (where price first enters from above during retracement)
Alternative: Market order when price is inside the overlap zone


STOP LOSS PLACEMENT:
→ Below the OB lower boundary (the bottom of the OB candle)
→ Buffer: 60–80 pips below the OB
→ This is the invalidation point: If price trades below
  the OB entirely, the Unicorn thesis is invalid


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (25%): 1:1 R (initial security)
T2 (25%): Previous swing high (before the BOS)
T3 (25%): 1:2.5 R or next H4 resistance
T4 (25%): Runner — trail using structure


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: OB and FVG have large overlap zone (100+ pips),
          TDS 10, the BOS was clean and decisive,
          DXY strongly falling, Daily bias bullish,
          the retracement to the Unicorn zone is the
          first retracement after the BOS (cleanest signal)


A Setup:  TDS 8–9, clear OB/FVG overlap, H4 bullish,
          BOS confirmed on H1


B Setup:  TDS 7, small overlap zone (less than 50 pips),
          BOS somewhat ambiguous → Skip or reduce size


XAUUSD SPECIFIC NOTES:
→ The Unicorn Model occurs most clearly after major
  gold directional moves — particularly post-news
→ After NFP or CPI creates a large bullish move,
  the first retracement back to the OB/FVG zone
  frequently creates a textbook Unicorn setup
→ Time the Unicorn within an active session window
  — it is much less reliable in the Asian session


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

SETUP 22: ICT UNICORN MODEL (BEARISH)

FREQUENCY: 1–2 times per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 50–58%

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
The bearish Unicorn Model. A bearish BOS occurs
(price breaks below a significant swing low). The
retracement back to the OB/FVG overlap zone (which
now acts as resistance) provides the short entry.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Clear bearish BOS on H1: Price breaks below significant
  swing low decisively
□ Price retraces back up toward the BOS origin
□ Identify the Bearish OB: The last BULLISH H1 candle

□ Identify the Bearish FVG: Within the BOS move downward

□ Price retraces INTO this overlap zone

□ H4/Daily: BEARISH bias

Stop: Above the OB upper boundary + 60–80 pip buffer

 SETUP 23 — ICT BREAKER BLOCK ENTRY


```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SETUP 23: ICT BREAKER BLOCK ENTRY
CATEGORY: ICT | DIFFICULTY: Advanced
FREQUENCY: 1 time per week
EXPECTED WIN RATE: 48–56%
MINIMUM R:R: 1:2 | OPTIMAL SESSION: London or NY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


SETUP OVERVIEW:
An ICT Breaker Block occurs when a significant Order
Block is violated — price trades through it. The OB,
having been broken, flips its function: a bullish OB
that is violated becomes resistance (bearish breaker).
A bearish OB that is violated becomes support (bullish
breaker). The entry is taken when price returns to
test the Breaker Block zone after violating the original OB.


IDENTIFICATION CRITERIA:
□ Identify a significant H1 or H4 OB
  (the last bullish or bearish candle before a swing move)
□ Price trades THROUGH and BEYOND the OB completely
  (violates the OB — the price moves past the full
   OB candle body, not just wicking through)
□ This creates the Breaker Block
□ Price then retraces back into the Breaker Block zone
  (the former OB zone now acting as opposing support/resistance)
□ Within the Breaker Block zone: An H1 reaction occurs
  (rejection candle or small CRT pattern)
□ TDS minimum: 8


ENTRY RULES:
Limit order at the Breaker Block zone boundary
Stop: Beyond the Breaker Block zone
      Buffer: 60–80 pips beyond the zone boundary


TARGET STRUCTURE:
T1 (33%): 1:1 R
T2 (33%): Next significant structural level in direction
T3 (33%): 1:3 R or H4 target


QUALITY GRADE CRITERIA:
A+ Setup: The OB that became the Breaker was a significant
          H4 OB (not just H1), TDS 10, clear violation
          of the original OB with conviction, clean
          return to the Breaker zone


A Setup:  TDS 8–9, H1 OB violated and tested, H4 aligned
B Setup:  TDS 7, ambiguous Breaker → Skip


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```
